English: The *boys* are playing ball.
*Roosters* crow at dawn.
How much does one rick of *wood* cost?
He’s thinking about raising *chicks.*
When he threw the ball down‚ it *bounced.*
I see a lame man *limping.*
The ball player is *bouncing* the ball hard.
*She put on* a pretty *belt *when she went to town.
I got a *belt* for Christmas.
The preacher asked for *prayers.*
*He prays* three times a day.
She bought *tender* green beans at the store.
*A very young* child drinks only milk.
*Love* is important.
Some pills are *killers* if too many are swallowed.
He gave his wife a new *purse.*

Syllabary: ᏓᏂᏍᏓᏲᎯᎲ ᎾᎥ ᏱᎩ ᏗᏓᏁᏗ ᎠᎾᏓᎾᏁᏒᎲᏍᎪ ᏴᏫ.
Transliteration: danisdayohihv nav yigi didahnehdi anadananesvhvsgo yvwi.
English: When it’s close to Christmas‚ people shop for *gifts.*
Tone: dà:ni:sdayo:hihv̋: na’v̋: yi̋gi di:da̋:hnehdi à:nada:nané:svhvsgó: yv:wi.

Syllabary: ᎤᏓᏃᏰᏗ ᎤᏚᎵ ᎠᏎᏃ Ꮭ ᎠᏓᏃᏲ ᏳᏩᏯ.
Transliteration: utanoyehdi uduli asehno hla atanoyo yuwaya.
English: *She* wants *to fan* herself‚ but she has no fan.
Tone: u:tano:yé:hdi̋ ù:du:lí a:sé:hnó: hla atano:yó yuwá:ya.

English: It’s hot. Plug in the *fan.*

Syllabary: Ꮭ ᎠᏴ ᎠᏇᏅᏒ ᏱᎩ ᎠᎭᏂ. ᎦᏓᏩᏛᎯᏙᎭᏊ.
Transliteration: hla aya agwenvsv yigi ahani gadawahtvhidohagwu
English: This isn’t my home. *I’m just visiting.*
Tone: hla aya à:gwe:nv́:sv̋ yigi áhani gadà:wahtv:hi:dô:hagwu

English: When he finished writing letters‚ *he stretched.*
He was cured of *cancer.*
When he was accused of stealing‚ *he denied it.*
*I am denying him‚* I just don’t know him.
The eighth commandment says‚ ’Thou shalt *not commit adultery.’*
The Bible reproves *adultery.*

Syllabary: ᏣᏂ ᎣᏍᏗ ᎧᏃᎮᏓ ᏣᏁᎵᏁ ᎠᏯᏙᏢ ᎠᏥᏁᎢᏍᏗ ᎠᏓᏲᏁᎯ ᎠᎨᏯ.
Transliteration: jani osdi kanoheda chaneline ayatohlv ajinesdi adahyonehi agehya.
English: An *adulteress* is mentioned in chapter eight of the book of John.
Tone: jǎ:ni ő:sdi kano:hě:da chane:li:ne̋: à:yâ:tohlv̋: à:jì:nê:sdí ada:hyo:ne̋:hi agě:hya.

English: An old cloth is easy *to rip.*

Syllabary: ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎤᏍᏗ ᏥᎪᏯ ᎠᏓᏨᎢᏍᎩ ᎤᏩᏨᏴ ᎤᏬᏰᏂ.
Transliteration: gohusdi usdi tsgoya adajvysgi uwajvhya uwoyeni.
English: Some kind of a *stinging* bug stung him on his hand.
Tone: go:hű:sdi ű:sdi tsgǒ:ya ada:jv̋:ysgi ù:wa:jv́:hya uwò:ye̋:ni.

English: When he’s getting ready to go somewhere‚ *he uses a mirror.*
She has a big *mirror* on the dresser.
When it’s time for them to play ball‚ the ball game *begins.*

Syllabary: ᏥᏈᏍᏓ ᎤᏓᎾᏁᏒᎲᏍᏗ ᎤᏚᎵ ᎠᏎᏃ ᏥᏈᏍᏗ ᏓᎬᏩᎵᏗᏓᎾ.
Transliteration: tsgwisda udananesvhvsdi uduli asehno tsgwisdi dagvwalhdidana.
English: She wants *to do* a lot of *shopping*‚ but the prices are too high.
Tone: tsgwi̋:sda u:da:nanè:svhv́sdi̋ ù:du:lí a:sé:hnó tsgwǐ:sdi dà:gv:walhdǐ:dá’na.

English: I work in a *store* in the summer.
When he heard the dog bark‚ *he ran.*
The red light just *changed.*
When he got tired of guarding the prisoner‚ his friend *took his place.*
The ball players always have *substitutes.*
He gave a *supply* of food to the hungry.
A tornado destroyed a *building.*
When the *heart* stops‚ a person dies.
What are *you thinking about?*
I’m just giving *you* something *to think about.*
*He’s considering* something I told him.
When you get close to a beaver‚ *it makes noise.*
A big snake *is crawling.*
When the twister hit the tree‚ *it twisted.*

Syllabary: ᏍᎪᎯ ᏧᏕᏘᏴᏓ ᎤᏁᎳᏛᎢ ᏓᎵᏆ. ᎩᎳ ᎤᏚᎸᎲ ᎤᏓᏅᏍᏗᎢ.
Transliteration: sgohi judetiyvda uneladv daligwa kila udulvhv udanvsdiyi
English: He lived in Tahlequah for ten years‚ then *he* wanted to *move.*
Tone: sgǒ:hi ju:de:tiyv̋:da ù:ně:ládv́: daligwa kila ù:du:lv:hv́ u:dá’nv̀:sdi̋:yi

English: *He orders* a new car every year.
She’s in the *kitchen* washing dishes.

Syllabary: Ꮭ ᎠᏎ ᏣᏓᏍᏓᏴᏗ ᏱᎩ‚ ᏝᎮᏃ ᏯᎩᏲᏏᎭ.
Transliteration: hla ase jadasdayhdi yigi hlahehno yagiyosiha.
English: You don’t have *to cook‚* because I’m not hungry.
Tone: hla a:sé jada̋:sdà:yhdi yigi hláhe:hnó yagíyò:sǐ:ha.

English: Some Cherokees like to talk about *magicians.*
Some Cherokees are very *magical.*
The match is *burning.*
The *flame* burned his back.
My dad works in a place where they make *gunpowder.*
*Rest.* You’ll get exhausted.
A man named *Adam* wants to speak to you.
Do *you* like to *swim?*

Syllabary: ᎤᏪᏴ ᎠᏆᏓᏬᏍᏗ ᎤᎪᏓ ᎠᎩᎸᏉᏓ ᏏᏃ ᎠᏓᏬᏍᏗᎨᏒᎢ.
Transliteration: uweyv agwadawosdi ugoda agilvkwda  sihno adawosdi gesvi.
English: I like to swim in a creek more than in a *swimming pool.*
Tone: ù:wé:yv̋: agwadawò:sdi̋: ù:gô:da à:gilv̌:kwda  si:hnó: adawò:sdi̋ gè:sv̋:’i.

English: Where did you get the color *television?*
*He gave* the student a new pencil.
Is it easy *to learn* to speak Cherokee?
She’s trying *to embarrass* him.
When the girl winked at him‚ *he got embarrassed.*

Syllabary: ᎯᏍᎩ ᏳᏩᏂᎸ ᏥᎨᏒ ᎪᎯ ᏑᎾᎴ ᎤᏕᏅᎢ.
Transliteration: hisgi yuwahnilv jigesv kohi sunale udehnvi
English: *He was born* at five o’clock this morning.
Tone: hisgi yúwa:hnilv̋: jigè:sv̋: kò:hi suná:le̋: ù:de:hnv̌:’i

English: The love of *money* is the root of evil.
Would you like to work in a *bank?*
She wants to go to *California.*
*He found out* that he was being cheated.
The price of this hat is ten *dollars.*
I like *to dive* when I’m swimming.
*I am circling* a building.
Because he was driving fast‚ he missed a *curve.*
*I am going to lay* this fishing pole here.
*I rise* at 6 a.m.

Syllabary: ᏗᎪᏪᎵ ᎦᏍᎩᎸ ᏚᏝᎥ ᏚᏗᎦᎴᏴ ᏗᎪᏪᎶᏗ ᎤᏯᎳ.
Transliteration: digohweli gasgilv duhlav dudigaleyv digohwelodi uhyala.
English: *He scattered* the papers on the table when he looked for his pencil.
Tone: digo:hwe:lî ga:sgilv̋ dù:hlá’v̋ dù:di:galé:yv digo:hwe̋:lò:dî ù:hyála.

English: Pieces of cloth *scattered* when the wind blew hard.
The wheel is *spinning* too fast to stop.
What *is he saying?*
Crossing the road‚ I was almost *run over* by a car.
When the killer saw the police *he hid.*

Syllabary: ᎯᏗᏏᏯ ᎦᏍᎩᎶ. Ꮭ ᏳᏜᏅᏓ ᎦᎳᏗᏍᏗ ᏓᏆᎴᎷᎢ.
Transliteration: hidisiya gasgilo hla yudlanvda galadisdi dagwalelui.
English: *Leave* the chair. There isn’t room to put it in the car.
Tone: hidì:sǐ:ya ga:sgiló hlá yu:dlánv:da galadì:sdi̋ dagwalé:lű:’i.

English: What kind of *drink* do you want?
Camels *drink* a lot of water.
He’s drinking soup with a *spoon.*
I see smoke from burning *rubber.*
The boy is *scratching* where a mosquito bit him.
The money is in the *coat pocket.*
Where *did he turn off?*

Syllabary: ᎤᏞᏤᏗ ᎤᏚᎸᎲ ᎠᏎᏍᎩᏂ Ꮭ ᎥᏍᎩ ᏱᏄᏛᏁᎴᎢ.
Transliteration: udlechehdi udulvhv asesgini hla vsgi yinudvnelei.
English: He wanted *to take revenge* against him but he didn’t do it.
Tone: u:dlè:chéhdi̋ ù:du:lv:hv́ a:sé:sginí hla v̀:sgi yinu:dv̂:ne:lé:’i.

English: Don’t be looking for *revenge.*
She puts on an *apron* when she works in the kitchen.
I didn’t see the *turn-off.*
What *is she pouring?* Water or milk?

Syllabary: ᏧᏬᏚᎯ ᏗᏟᎠᏙ ᏚᏂᎭ ᎠᏂᏣᎳᎩ ᎤᏃᏢᏅᏅ ᎤᎾᏓᎾᏅᎢ ᏓᎵᏆ.
Transliteration: juwoduhi didliado duniha anijalagi unohlvhnvnv unadananvi daligwa.
English: They have some pretty *earrings* at the Cherokee Arts and Crafts in Tahlequah.
Tone: juwo:dű:hi di:dlí’adó dù:nǐ:ha ani:jalagi u:no:hlv:hnv:nv̋: u:nada:ná’nv̋:’i dalígwa.

English: While I was *swimming‚* water filled my ear.
*He’s drawing* water from a barrel.
What *is she pouring it into?* A glass or a bottle?
I want five oil *containers.*
Don’t *cry.*

Syllabary: ᎠᎦᏴᎵᏳ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏎᎦᏨ ᎤᏠᎢᏒ ᏐᏈᎵᎢ.
Transliteration: agayvliyu iyusdi gesv segajv udloysv sogwilii.
English: Because of his old age‚ *he* barely *got off* his horse.
Tone: agayv:lǐ:yú: iyű:sdi gè:sv̋: se:gà:jv̋: ù:dlô:ysv́ sǒ:gwíli̋:’i.

English: All he does is *just brag* when he speaks.
Do you think *you* can *catch up with him?*
He makes *walking canes* by carving wood.
Do you know anyone who is an apprentice *sorcerer?*
Where does one find *freedom*?
This *German* is my friend.
What *will you be doing* Saturday?
*He’s acting silly* because he wants to be laughed at.
The student is studying to become an *actor.*
*I’m getting ready* to go somewhere.
*She prepares* a lot of food at Christmas.
When children play on the ground‚ they become *dirty.*
*He’s* always *eager* to help.

Syllabary: ᎤᏍᎪᏍᏗ ᎠᎦᎵᏍᎪ ᏅᏓ ᎢᏳᏃ ᎧᎵ ᏅᏓ ᏱᎩ.
Transliteration: usgosdi agalisgo nvda iyuhno kali nvda yigi.
English: The moon *shines* brightly if it’s a full moon.
Tone: u:sgő:sdi à:gá:li:sgó nv:da iyú:hnó kali̋: nv:da yi̋gi.

English: The girl wants *to cut* her hair.
*He broke* the string with his hands.

Syllabary: ᎢᎦᏓ ᎠᎦᎷᎦ ᏧᏍᏗ ᎨᏐ. ᎢᎦᏓᏃ ᏧᏔᎾ.
Transliteration: igada agaluga jusdi geso igadahno jutana.
English: Some *twisters* are small and some are big.
Tone: i:gá:da a:galǔ:ga ju:sdi̋: gè:só i:gá:dáhnó: jű:tana.

English: Cows like *to lick* salt.
I was in the *doctor’s* office.
*He’s warming* his feet.
When it becomes summer‚ *it gets warm.*

Syllabary: ᏥᏈᏍᏗ ᎠᎦᏍᎦ Ꮲ ᎡᏅᏍᏗ ᎪᎯ ᎢᎦ.
Transliteration: tsgwisdi agasga tlv envsdi kohi iga.
English: *It’s raining* too much to go somewhere today.
Tone: tsgwì:sdi à:ga:sga tlv̋: e:nv̀:sdi kò:hi i:ga.

English: This man is a specially *selected* speaker.
Cherokees use a *seine* to catch crawdads.

Syllabary: ᎠᎦᏴᎵ ᏌᎶᎵ ᏧᏲ ᎩᏟ ᎤᏩᎧ ᎡᏚᏥ.
Transliteration: agayvli saloli juhyo gihli unahla eduji.
English: My uncle has an *old* squirrel dog.
Tone: agayv̋:li salǒ:li jű:hyo gi:hli ù:nâ:hla e:du:ji.

English: The *old woman* has been teaching for twenty years.
I am going *over there* for awhile.
Who is that *woman?*
Cherokee *girls* like to play ball.

Syllabary: ᏧᏙᏓᏋᏓ ᏎᎷ ᎦᏚ ᎤᏂᎩᏍᏗ ᎤᏂᎸᏉᏓ ᎠᏂᏣᎳᎩ.
Transliteration: judodagwvda selu gadu unigisdi unilvkwda anijalagi.
English: The Cherokees like *to eat* corn bread every day.
Tone: ju:dǒ:dágwv̋:da se:lú gá:du u:ni:gì:sdi̋ u:ni:lv:kwda ani:jalagi.

English: When he saw money on the ground‚ *he picked it up.*

Syllabary: ᏔᎵ ᎢᏧᏕᏘᏴᏓ ᎢᏳᏓᎵ ᎢᏤ ᏓᏆᎴᎳ ᎠᎩᏍᎪᎢ.
Transliteration: tali ijudetiyvd yudali ije dagwalela agisgoi
English: He *gets* a new car every two years.
Tone: tá’li ijú:de:tiyv̋:d yú:da̋:li ije̋: dagwalé:la à:gí:sgó:’i

English: The Trail of Tears was *agony* for the Cherokees.
*He was in pain* for two days.
My horse has a *colt.*
Get *something to eat* at the store.
The woman always *chews* gum.

Syllabary: ᏍᎪᎯ ᎢᏯᏂᎢ ᏥᏔᎦ ᎠᏂᎩᏏ ᏕᏥᎪᏩᏘᎭ‚ ᏌᏊᏃ ᎠᏨᏯ.
Transliteration: sgohi iyani jitaga anigisi dejigowhtiha sagwuhno achvya.
English: I see ten *hens* and one rooster.
Tone: sgǒ:hi i:yáni̋: jitǎ:ga ani:gǐ:si de:jígowhtí:ha sà:gwű:hnó: achv:ya.

English: *He’s burning* old papers.
When the car overturned it started *burning.*
*Perch* are good to eat.
*I am reading* a letter from a friend.
The patient wanted a doctor *to examine him.*
Have you gone through *famine?*
He’s *target practicing* with a new bow.
Rabbits ate up her *lettuce.*
The lion ate some *raw* meat.
Don’t *you see it?*

Syllabary: ᎢᏤ ᎦᏄᎸᏒ ᏙᎯᏳ ᎤᏬᏚᎯ ᎠᎪᏩᏛᏗ ᎨᏐᎢ.
Transliteration: ije ganulvsv dohiyu uwoduhi agohwahtvhdi gesoi.
English: The green grass is really a beautiful *sight.*
Tone: ije̋: ganu:lv̂:sv̋: do:híyű uwo:dű:hi agő:hwahtvhdi gè:só:’i.

English: Your mother wants *to see* a picture of you.
This boy is a Creek Indian.

Syllabary: ᎦᏂᏱᏍᎩ ᏍᏆᏞᏍᏗ ᎦᏂᏱᏍᎬ ᏚᏅᏓ ᎠᎬᏓᎨᏂ ᎤᏩᏂᎸᎢ.
Transliteration: ganiyisgi sgwahlesdi ganiyisgv dunvda agvdageni uwahnilvi.
English: When the catcher missed catching the ball‚ it hit him on *his forehead.*
Tone: ga’nǐ:yi̋:sgi sgwà:hle̋:sdi gá’ni:yí:sgv̋: dù:nv̋:da agv:dage̋:ni ù:wa:hnilv̌:’i.

English: *She cut* a pattern for a dress.
*She’s slicing* the cake with a knife.
*Chop up* some cabbage to cook.
Give me *a piece of cake.*
Is your friend a white person or *black?*
The man *is scolding* his son.
A cat *washes its face* after it eats.
Nobody likes *a loudmouth.*
*Flag* that car *down.*
Let’s meet *here* after a while.
*Deer* meat is good to eat.
When I closed the windows‚ *I smothered it.*

Syllabary: ᎢᎦᏓ ᎢᎾᏓ Ꮭ ᎠᎯᏓ ᏱᎩ ᏗᎪᏩᏛᏗ.
Transliteration: igada inada hla ahida yigi digowahtvhdi.
English: Some snakes are not *easy* to see.
Tone: i:gá:da i:nada hla ahi̋:da yigi di:go:wahtv́hdi̋.

English: What do *you have in your hand?*
*I killed* seven squirrels when I went hunting.
*He’s driving* his dad’s car.
He’s *driving* the cattle toward the pasture.
Something happened to the *steering wheel.*
*Leave* the car when you go back.
*Put* the bottle on the table.
When something scares a bird‚ *it flies away.*
Do you want *something that flies?*
He likes *to fly* a toy airplane.
Are you *the one who flies* this plane?
When he has eaten a lot‚ *he has a nightmare.*
How long *did it take* for you to get a haircut?

Syllabary: ᏗᏁᏍᎨᏍᎩ ᎠᏟᎶᏍᏗ ᎬᏗᏍᎪ ᎠᏟᎶᏍᎬ ᎢᏴ ᎤᏍᏆᎵᏍᏗ ᏯᏖᎾ.
Transliteration: dihnesgesgi ahlilosdi gvhdisgo ahlilosgv iyv usgwalsdi hyahtena.
English: A carpenter uses a ruler *in measuring* the place where he cut the board.
Tone: di:hnè:sge̋:sgi ahli:lő:sdi gvhdi:sgô: à:hli:ló:sgv̋: iyv̋: u:sgwà:lsdi̋: hyahtě:na.

English: Hand me a *ruler.*

Syllabary: ᏓᏆᎴᎳ ᎬᏗ ᎤᏩᏂᎳ ᏔᎷᎩᏍᎩ ᎨᏛ‚ ᎤᏟᏴᏈᏛᏅᎢ.
Transliteration: dagwalela gvhdi uwahnila talugisgi getv uhliyvhgwidvnvi
English: When he hit the steel post with a car‚ *it bent.*
Tone: dagwalé:la gv̋hdi ù:wa̋:hnila talu:gì:sgi ge:tv̋ ù:hliyvhgwidv̂:nv̌:’i

English: When a deer is trying to scare something‚ *it snorts.*
He *preached* for one hour.

Syllabary: ᏦᎢ ᎢᏯᏂ ᏧᏪᏥ ᎠᏂᏧᏣ ᎠᎾᎵᏣᏙᎲᏍᎩ ᏄᎾᎵᏍᏔᏅᎢ.
Transliteration: joi iyani juweji anichuja analhjadohvsgi nunalstanvi
English: Three of his sons became *preachers.*
Tone: jo’i i:yáni̋: juwě:ji ani:chű:ja analhjadő:hvsgi nù:nalstanv́:’i

English: When a ball hit the window‚ *it shattered.*
He *shatters* the bottles by shooting them.
I like to play *records.*
When we played ball by ourselves‚ *I beat him.*

Syllabary: ᏂᎪᎯᎸ ᎠᎵᏔᏬᏍᎪᎢ‚ ᎥᏍᎩᏅ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎤᏬᏚᎯ ᏧᏍᏘᎦ.
Transliteration: nigohilv alhtawosgo vsgihno iyusdi uwoduhi justihga.
English: *She combs her hair* all the time‚ and that’s why she has pretty hair.
Tone: nigo:hí:lv̋: alhtawó:sgó: v̀:sgihnó: iyű:sdi uwo:dű:hi jù:stíhga.

English: I want to buy *a comb.*

Syllabary: Ꮭ ᎤᎵᏬᏨ ᏱᎩ Ꮎ ᎢᎾᏓ. Ꮟ ᎠᎵᏖᎸᎲᏍᎦᏊ.
Transliteration: hla ulihwojv yigi na inada si alhtelvhvsgagwu
English: The snake isn’t dead. *It’s* still *moving.*
Tone: hla u:li:hwò:jv̋: yigi ná i:nada sí à:lhté:lv:hv́sgagwu

English: When his shirt got dirty‚ *he changed it.*
When it got warm‚ *he took off* his coat.
I have *a shirt* made of cowhide.

Syllabary: ᎢᏤ ᎦᏌᎴᏂ ᎠᏥᏅᏁᎸ ᎠᏎᏃ Ꮭ ᏳᎿᏬᎡᎢ.
Transliteration: ije gasaleni ajinvnelv asehno hla yuhnawoei
English: She was given a new coat‚ but *she* didn’t *wear it.*
Tone: ije̋: gà:salě:ni à:jinv́:ne:lv́ a:sé:hnó hla yu:hnáwo:’é’i

English: *He gave* his son ten dollars.
Indians like *to wrestle.*
*He’s interpreting* the speaker’s speech.
I want a *translation* of this letter.
The children *are frolicking* in the grass.

Syllabary: Ꮎ ᏗᏁᏍᎨᏍᎩ ᎤᏔᎾ ᏥᏔᎩ ᎤᏂᏴᏍᏗ ᎤᏁᏍᎨᎲᎢ.
Transliteration: na dihnesgesgi utana jitagi uniyvsdi uhnesgehvi
English: The carpenter *built* a big chicken house.
Tone: ná di:hnè:sge̋:sgi ű:tana jitǎ:gi u:ni:yv:sdi̋: ù:hné:sge:hv̌:’i

English: *He left* yesterday morning.

Syllabary: ᎢᏤ ᎠᎾᎦᎵᏍᎩ ᎤᏝᎾ‚ ᎠᎯᏓ ᎤᏂᎩᏒ ᏓᏆᎴᎳ.
Transliteration: ije anagalisgi uhlana ahida uhnigisv dagwalela.
English: When he put a new battery in the car‚ *it started* up easily.
Tone: ije̋: anà:galǐ:sgi ù:hlána ahi̋:da ù:hnígí:sv́ dagwalé:la.

Syllabary: ᏓᏆᎴᎳ ᎤᏂᎩᏍᏙᏗ ᎤᏚᎵ‚ ᎠᏎᏅ Ꮭ ᏗᏍᏚᎢᏍᏗ ᏱᏚᏩᏯ.
Transliteration: dagwalela uhnigisdohdi uduli asehno hla disduisdi yiduwaya.
English: He wants *to start* the car‚ but he doesn’t have the keys.
Tone: dagwalé:la u:hnígì:sdohdi̋ ù:du:lí a:sé:hnó hla di:sdu’i̋:sdi yiduwá:ya.

English: He carried three posts *on his shoulder.*
He burned *his mouth* drinking hot soup.
*She’s putting* some corn *in* the boiling water.
Where can I find some *stove pipes?*

Syllabary: ᏍᏗᎩᏓᏊ ᎠᎦᏍᎦᏂ ᏗᏜ ᏳᏗᏣᎧᎿᎾ ᏳᏫᎪᏩᏔ ᎠᎱᏣᏬᎳᏛᎢ.
Transliteration: sdikidagwu aksgani didla yudijakahnana yuhwigohwahta ahujawoldvi
English: If you will look just a little to the left‚ you will see a *chimney.*
Tone: sdi̋:kidagwu aksgáni dí:dla yu:dijákahnana yuhwigo:hwahta à:hu:jawǒ:ldv̋:’i

English: Indians use *a drum* when they chant.
He wanted to *hit him on the mouth*‚ but he got scared.
*Move* the car. It’s in the way.
*Set* the groceries *down.*
When something is hidden from him‚ *he finds it* easily.
Who do you want *to visit?*
*He took* the ball home with him.
*She planted* flowers near the building.
Do you want to buy a *necktie?*
*Peel* some potatoes.
She received a pretty *necklace* for her birthday.
*Did it frost* last night?
*He’s calling* the dog with a cow horn.
Where did *you get* this dog?
Where *are you taking* the ball?

Syllabary: ᏍᎪᎯ ᎠᏕᎸ ᏧᎬᏩᏟᏗ ᏌᏊ ᎢᏯᎾᏓᏅᏖᏗ ᏓᏰᎲᎢ.
Transliteration: sgohi adelv junigvwalhdi sagwu iyanadanhtehdi dahyehvi
English: He had ten dollars worth of pennies *in his hand.*
Tone: sgo:hí ade:lv̋: ju:nigv̋:walhdi sà:gwű iyánada̋:nhtehdi dà:hye:hv̌:’i

English: *She quilted it* with white cotton.
The surgeon *is operating on* the patient.
He *imitates* birds with his whistling.
Who are you *mocking?*
I lost my *knife* while hunting.
*I’m looking for* my friend. Where did he go?
How far *can you swim?*
Do you hear it *thundering?*
Are you afraid of thunder?
He wants to buy an *air conditioner* in the summer.
He made a six-foot *ladder.*
Don’t *bend it!*
When I finish sewing the quilt‚ *I am going to hem it.*
I was going to shoot a rabbit in *his throat* but I missed.
He tried *to kick* above his head.
Some soldiers have become *prisoners.*
The solider *captured* his enemy.
Who *hit him on the nose?*
The shirt collar *is choking him.*
Someone *is walking toward* the building.
How many *fish* did you catch?
Is that man a *Choctaw* or a Cherokee?
How many chicks *hatched?*
When he goes to the city‚ *he dresses up.*
*Get in* the car.
She put flowers on the *grave.*
He’s *acting like* an Indian.
The boys were wrestling on the *mattress.*
*He’s spilling* water on the ground.
When he’s got a lot of money‚ *he just wastes it.*
At the stomp grounds‚ they dance around the *fire.*
Let’s go look for some watercress.
A *Catholic* spoke at the meeting.
Give me a *match.*
He threw coal *into the fire.*
Flowers *bloom* in the spring.
Someone gave her some *flowers.*
A *cedar* is always green.
*He lit* the cigarette with a match.
*He’s blowing* on the hot coffee.
*Turn on the lights* at six o’clock in the morning.
When the lightning flashed‚ the electric *lights* went out.
The policeman was given a *badge*.
He hurt *his hip* when he fell.
When the dog sees cows‚ *he chases them*.
Where are the *stairs?*
He likes to ride *a bicycle.*
The doctor gave the patient some *pills.*
*I am* barely *swallowing* because my throat hurts.
I saw an *elbow* through an opening in the door.
*His elbows* got dirty when he crawled.
*She’s shovelling* a place to set out flowers.
He’s making a handle for a *shovel.*

Syllabary: ᎠᎦᏘᏏ ᏗᏜ ᏭᏞᏒ ᎠᏂᏐᎢᏃ ᎠᎦᏍᎦᏂ ᏗᏜ ᏭᎾᏞᏒᎢ.
Transliteration: aktisi didla wudlesv anisoihno aksgani didla wunadlesvi.
English: He turned right but the others turned *left.*
Tone: aktǐ:si dí:dla wù:dlé:sv́ ani̋:so’íhnó: aksganí dí:dla wù:nadlé:sv̌:’i.

English: This ball player is *left-handed.*
*He goes down a* long *hill* when he goes after water.
He pushed the car *down hill.*
He has one artificial *eye.*
*Do you know how* to speak Cherokee?
Who is a *witness* to what happened?
This man is very *knowledgeable.*
When he heard someone calling him‚ *he turned around.*
He wanted to turn back before he got there.
The boy *is winking* at the girl.
*He hit* the boy *in the eye* with a rock.
The *scenery* is pretty in the spring.
I don’t want anything. *I’m just looking around.*
Someone *is peeking* through the window.
He throws a ball with *his right hand.*
*Wait for me.* I’ll be right back.
He has chosen to be a *guard* at the bank.
He wants a *telescope* for his birthday.
*He became wise* when he was just a boy.
*His* right *eye* is always bothering him.
Do *you* like *to watch* television?
Some Cherokees have *wisdom.*
He barely *went through* the small opening.
*She’s dipping* water from a bucket.
Where’s the *dipper?* I’m thirsty (for water).
*I’m paying* sixty dollars a month for my car.
How much *did you pay him?*
Cherokees like feather *pillows.*
He killed the snake *by tromping on it.*
*He* always *speaks badly of* people.
There’s a bug crawling. *Step on it!*
He didn’t *play ball* because his hand was hurting.
The *ballplayers* practice every day.
A crippled man *takes a step* carefully.
The lawyer began working on a *law suit.*
He forgot to polish the *shoes.*
As I walked along the grass‚ I *almost *stepped on a snake.
I see men *and* women.

Syllabary: ᏤᏅᏍᏗ ᏱᏣᏚᎵ‚ ᎠᎴ Ꮭ ᏱᎩ ᎣᏏᏊ.
Transliteration: jenvsdi yijaduli ale la yigi osigwu.
English: If you want to go *or* not‚ that’s just fine.
Tone: je:nv̀:sdi̋ yijadú:li̋ ale la yi̋gi o:sǐ:gwu.

English: He tried *to stand* a heavy piece of steel *in an upright position.*
*Stand here* in the middle.
When he heard someone calling him‚ he *stopped.*
*Stop* the car.
When can *you start* working?
His shirt was soaked with *sweat.*
The coffee *is boiling.*

Syllabary: Ꮎ ᎦᏅᎯᏓ ᎤᏍᏘᎩ ᏥᎩ ᎠᏴᏫᏯ ᎠᎵᎬᏓᏃᏍᏙᏗ ᎤᏚᎵᎭ.
Transliteration: na ganvhida ustihgi jigi ayvwiya algvdanosdohdi uduliha.
English: The Indian with the long hair wants a *headband.*
Tone: ná ganv:hi̋:da ű:stihgi ji̋gi ayv:wiyá algv:danő:sdohdi ù:du:lǐ:ha.

English: Firemen use protective *masks.*
When his son helps him work‚ *he thanks him.*
For what reason *is he happy?*
*Friendship* is important in the lives of people.
*Let’s trade* knives.
I caught a rabbit yesterday‚ but *it ran away from me.*
*He escaped* from jail.
I lost some *gloves.*
When he goes swimming‚ *he takes his ring off.*
I am making a big *ring.*
She has a three hundred dollar ring on her finger.
He has on red *socks.*
Don’t go *around spitting.*
*Raise up* awhile.
She wants to shop for *groceries.*
In the evening *he eats* a big *meal.*
The student asked for *assistance.*
*He helps* in the building of houses.
When he entered the house‚ *he took off his cap.*
How long does it take *when she washes her hair?*
*Put on a cap* when you go out because it’s cold outside.
Where did you get this *cap?*
*He sat down* close to where the speaker was standing.
When something hit him in the eye‚ *he shook his head.*
Some Cherokee girls like *to dance.*
*Permit me* to speak.
*Nod your head* if you accept.
I had just arrived when their speeches *ended.*
A little boy *is sticking his head out* the window.
*Take your hat off.* Let me look at it.

Syllabary: ᏗᎦᏚᎲ ᏳᏪᎾ ᏧᏓᏅᎦᎸᏓ ᏓᎿᏬᏍᎪᎢ ᎣᏍᏓᏃ ᎠᎵᏍᏇᏚᏬ ᎠᎵᏍᏇᏚᎲᏍᎪᎢ.
Transliteration: digaduhv yuwena judanvgalvda dahnawosgoi osdahno alsgwetuwo alsgwetuhvsgoi
English: When he goes to town‚ *he puts on* clean clothes and *a* good *hat.*
Tone: diga:dú:hv̋: yuwe̋:na ju:danv:galv̋:da dà:hnawó:sgó:’i ő:sdahnó: álsgwè:tuwó à:lsgwé:tuhvsgó:’i

English: How much did you pay for the *hat?*
What *is he aiming* to do?
*She* likes *to smell* flowers.
We get our drinking *water* from a spring.
Get some *salt* when you go to town.
Who dug this *water well?*
Fill the *waterbucket.*
My home is in the *United States.*
Jump *into* the *water.*
I found these shoes by the *shore.*
The fish in the *ocean* are big.
She entered a beauty *contest.*

Syllabary: ᏥᏈᏍᏓ ᏴᏫ ᎠᎾᏓᏟᏏᎭ ᏗᎦᏚᎲ ᎪᎯ ᎢᎦ.
Transliteration: tsgwisda yvwi anadahlisiha digaduhv kohi iga.
English: A lot of people *are gathering* in town today.
Tone: tsgwi̋:sda yv:wi à:nadá:hli:si:ha diga:dú:hv̋: kò:hi i:ga.

English: It’s *lightening* too much to play outside.
The dead *battery* couldn’t start the car.

Syllabary: ᎯᎠ ᎯᏍᎩ ᎢᏯᏂᎢ ᎠᏂᏧᏣ ᏂᎦᏓ ᎠᎾᏟᏅᏟ.
Transliteration: hia hisgi yani anichuja nigada anahlinvhlv
English: These five boys are all *brothers.*
Tone: hi’a hisgi yani̋: ani:chű:ja niga̋:da à:nahlinv́:hlv́

English: This boy and girl want *to go together.*
Little girls play with *dolls.*
*He’s trying* to start the car.
My friend *got married* as soon as he turned eighteen.
*Do you remember* what I said long ago?
They grow a lot of *strawberries* in Stilwell.
*They were standing* for two hours.
At eleven o’clock last night‚ *they were* already *asleep.*
*They sat* quietly.
*Her husband* teaches at school.
I’m going to *Mexico* in the summer.
I was in the *military service* for three years.
My mother’s birthday is *May* fifth.
Spring begins in the month of *March.*
*He set a trap* for a raccoon but he didn’t catch it.
*Raise* the window.
Who is *smarter*‚ you or your sister?
I want a new *dress.*
*She’s starching* her husband’s shirts.
When he put the snake skin out in the sunshine‚ *it became crisp.*
*Follow* these lines.
He *drew a line* with a red pencil.
The little boy *is following* his father.
*I am helping him* build a house.
The girl *is braiding* her hair.
*He* wants *to eat* a cucumber.
A pencil is not *something to eat.*
Do you want *him to crush* some rocks?
What happened to the *crusher?*
The barber trims his son’s hair.
*Close* the door.
If someone is drunk‚ *he jails him.*
*He opens* the store at seven o’clock.
He ate three *sandwiches.*
*He sandwiched it* between the bread.
Do you think it’s going to snow? *Maybe.*
I felt *peculiar* for awhile.
When he gets here *show him* where to park the car.
I was going to go outside *but* it’s too cold.
He forgot *to include* my friend.
*He withdrew* his candidacy for president.
The motion has been *withdrawn.*
When the boy broke the window‚ his father *reprimanded him.*
*I’m afraid* of that big dog.
The dog tried *to bite* the man.
What is the biggest *sin?*
Is there anyone living who is not a *sinner?*
He doesn’t want *to commit a crime.*
Do you recognize that *man?*
The woman said‚ ’My husband is *handsome.’"
An *officer*is going to inspect the soldiers’ uniforms.
What is the difference between a *devil* and Satan?
What did *you dream* about last night?
A beaver makes a tree fall by *gnawing it.*
The little boy *mourned for* a dead dog.
*Rub* my head‚ it hurts.
*He erased* his name on the paper.
Hand me those *erasers.*
The pretty color on the shirt *is fading.*
*I am digging* a water well.
He rubbed *his shins* when he stopped running.
*He finishes* his work at 4:30.
*His side* is hurting from running.
When the ball was thrown to him‚ *he caught it.*
He found a *short* pencil.
Whenever you sharpen a pencil‚ *it becomes shorter.*
The man *broke* an axe handle.
*He* tried *to hit* the dog *on the head* with a bat.
A lot of *Mexicans* live in Texas.

Syllabary: ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏒ ᎾᎯᏗᏴ ᎤᏕᏠᏆᎥ ᎤᎪᎵᏰᏗ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎠᏧᏣ.
Transliteration: usgwanigosv nahidiyv udehlohgwav ugoliyedi uweji achuja.
English: *He was amazed* at how fast his son learned to read.
Tone: ù:sgwanigo:sv́: nà:hi:dí:yv̋: ù:de:hlohgwá’v̋: u:go:lǐ:yê:di̋ uwě:ji achű:ja.

English: *She’s sucking* on a ripe tomato.
When the dog attacked him‚ *he hit it in the stomach* with a stick.
*He placed his hand on* his friend’s shoulder.
*He slapped me* for no reason.
When I say back up‚ *back up.*
Why are you walking *backwards?*
*One person* spoke at the meeting.
I shot a snake *in the head* with an arrow.
*He roped* a wild horse.
*He built* a new *fence* around the garden.
The rooster is sitting on the *fence.*
When his friend passed by‚ *he waved to him.*
*Press down on it* hard.
The man *was wounded* fighting in a war.
How long a *string* do you want?
*I am sopping* gravy.
Where *is he fishing?*
What color *are you going to paint* the house?
Whenever he is going to work in the garden‚ *he changes his pants.*

Syllabary: ᎠᏏᎳᏛ ᎠᏓᏬᎯᎳᏗᏍᎬ ᎤᏲᏍᏔᎾ ᎤᏑᎶ ᎤᏑᎴᏒ ᎣᏍᏓ ᏄᏩᏁᎸᎢ.
Transliteration: asiladv adawohiladisgv uyostana usulo usulesv osda nuwanelvi.
English: When he tore his pants while crossing over a fence‚ *he took them off* and mended them.
Tone: asǐ:ládv̋: adawo:híladì:sgv̋: ù:yostana u:su:ló ú:su:lé:sv́ ő:sda nù:wá:ne:lv̌:’i.

English: *He put on* someone else’s *pants.*
When *did you quit* smoking?
What length of *pants* do you wear?
Do you know how to make *gravy?*
*Choose* the one that you want.
*Stir* the soup.

Syllabary: ᏒᎩ ᎢᎾᎨ ᎡᎯ ᏳᏅᏂᏍᏔᎾ ᎠᏂᏣᎳᎩ‚ ᏧᏪᏥ ᏓᎾᏑᏴᏍᎪᎢ.
Transliteration: svgi inage ehi yunvhnistana anijalagi juweji danasuyvsgoi
English: When Cherokees cook wild onions‚ *they mix them with* eggs.
Tone: sv̀:gi ǐ:náge̋ e̋:hi yu:nv́:hnistana ani:jalagi juwě:ji dà:nasǔ:yv́sgo’i

English: Something *smells* bad.
Do you know anyone who works in a *sawmill?*
When it flooded‚ the water destroyed some of the *bridges.*
He burned his hand *when he touched* a hot stove.
*It used up* one gallon of oil.
*He drank up* a glass full of milk.
My grandmother was *a Nighthawk.*
This boy likes to talk to *young women.*
Do you know what *rage* is?
*He held his breath* for two minutes.
*Moor* the boat when you’re through fishing.
*He drilled a hole* in the board with a drill.

Syllabary: ᏅᏯ ᏧᎾᏍᏗ ᎬᏗ ᏘᎧᎵᏣ ᏓᏔᎴᏒ ᎤᏃᎯ.
Transliteration: nvya junsdi gvhdi tikalija datalesv nvnohi.
English: Fill the *holes* in the road with gravel.
Tone: nv̀:ya ju:nsdi̋: gv̋hdi tikáli:ja dà:tale:sv̋: nv:nǒ:hi.

English: When you heat water to its boiling point‚ *it steams.*
How old is your *teenage* daughter?
When he threw the gunpowder into the fire‚ *it exploded.*
On July fourth they *fire* firecrackers.
It’s dangerous to smoke near a place where *explosives* are stored.
*She kissed* her daughter on the forehead.
*She’s combing* her son’s *hair.*
Don’t be afraid *to ask for it.*
He wants *to join* the military service.
She is washing *dishes.*
Don’t *shake* the ladder.
She made *a shirt* for her husband to wear.
*He’s leading* a blind man.
Why do *you* like *to argue?*
He’s always looking for an *argument.*
Someone *is running in this direction.*
Who would win *if you two raced?*

Syllabary: Ꮎ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᏥᎦᏬᏂ ᎠᎹᏰᏟ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏱᎦᎵᏍᏗᏍᎩ ᎠᏙᎩᏯᏍᎩ.
Transliteration: na asgaya jigawoni amayehli ugvwiyuhi yigalsdisgi atohgiyasgi
English: The man making the speech is a *candidate* for president of the United States.
Tone: ná asgaya jigawǒ:ni̋ amayé:hli u:gv:wiyǔ:hi yigálsdi̋:sgi atohgi̋:yà:sgi

English: Is this camera *borrowed?*
Who *loaned him* the car?
How long does *he* want to *borrow it?*
I see something *flashing.*
He *took a bite* of corn bread.
Are you lying to me?
He doesn’t like *to take a drink.*
He gave him *a drink.*
*He promised* to come over next month.
*I listened to him* speak for ten minutes.
*Ask him* what he’s going to do.
*He hears* only when you speak loud.
*She grows* beautiful flowers.
*He is* really *growing.*
Do you recognize that *young man?*
I didn’t know you had two *teenage boys.*
*Eagles* build their nests on mountain tops.
Which room *is he in?*
He was walking *fast.*
*He distributed* food among a lot of people.
He became a *deacon* in ’73.
Which *chapter* are you reading?
When he was told to *wake up‚ he woke up.*
Sit here *in the middle.*
Don’t *wake* the baby.
He burned half of *his body.*
*He put* the horse *inside* the barn.
Have you heard a hen *crowing?*
*He got* five new pencils at the store.
She *picked up* a pencil from the ground.
She accidentally spilled water on the *floor.*
The bottle *broke* when it dropped.
The hunter *shot* a deer with an arrow.
What time do you *take a break* from your work?
The *children* are playing ball.
He picked up some *small* rocks.
Several men *are hewing* railroad ties.
No one can hide from *death.*
When did *he die?*
*He broke up* a fight between the little boys.
Every time he seems me *he greets me.*
*He accidentally broke* a water glass.
I was *a soldier* for three years.
The strong wind *destroyed* the small building.
A *crippled man* asked for a walking cane.
Do you want *me* to go?
*I entered* the house when the rain came.
The boy *found* a new bat.
Where *are you taking* the wood?
There is a gate at the *entrance* to the pasture.
What are the *people* doing?
This boy is a full-blood *Indian.*
Have you heard a *white person* speak Cherokee?
Give him *eight* dollars.
He burns *coal* in his heating stove.
Do you have some *flea* killer?

Syllabary: ᏚᏙᎥ ᎤᏬᏪᎶᏗ ᎠᏍᏓᏅᏅ ᏚᏓᎿᏫᏍᏔᏅᎢ ᎾᎦᏔᎲᎾ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎩᎵᏏ ᎤᏬᏪᎶᏗᎢ.
Transliteration: dudov uwohwelodi asdanvhnv dudahnawstanv naktahvna gesv iyusd gilisi uwohwelodiyi.
English: *He made an X* on the signature line because he didn’t know how to write English.
Tone: dù:dó:’v̋: uwo:hwě:lô:dí à:sdanv:hnv̋ dù:da:hnâ:wstanv́ náktahv̋:na gè:sv̋ iyű:sd gili:sî uwo:hwě:lô:di̋:yi.

English: They met at a *crossroads.*
He looked for his conjuring material when *he* was asked to *conjure.*
Do you think *he is going to accept?*
Why didn’t *you dodge?*
The preacher *performed baptism* on a cold day.
He lasted a long time‚ then he *gave up.*
*She teaches* summer school.

Syllabary: ᏩᏍᏆᎳᎯᏴ ᎤᏓᏡᎬ ᎦᎸᏉᏗ ᎪᏪᎵ‚ ’ᏥᏌ ᏚᏠᏱᎸᎢ‚’ ᎠᏗᎭ.
Transliteration: wasgwalahiyv udahlgv galvkwdi gohweli jisa dudlohyilvi adiha.
English: The shortest verse in the bible says‚ ’Jesus *wept’.*
Tone: wasgwálahiyv̋: ù:dâ:hlgv̋: galv̋:kwdi go:hwe:li ’jǐ:sa dù:dlo:hyílv́:’i’ à:dǐ:ha.

English: *They were married* in a chapel last month.
They make necklaces out of *pearls.*
Whatever *price you set* I’ll pay you.
When he gets to town‚ the man is going to buy *a car.*
She saw a rocking chair *rocking* by itself.
*He drew a picture of* a bear.
*I was conversing* on the phone.
How high can *you jump?*
Do you think that *war* will ever cease?
*He changed* his wet *clothes.*
*I am undressing* to go to sleep.
*Put on* some clean *clothes.*
There is a stompground near *Vian.*
*He put* green wood *into the stove.*
Don’t *tickle* me while I am eating.
Which one is *better?*
He couldn’t get up from *a squatting position.*
*He is tying* my shoe laces *for me.*
*He untied* his shoes when he took them off.
Who is *looking after you?*
If you’re in the dark for a long time‚ *you’ll become blind.*
I saw *a terrapin* crossing the road.
He opened the *lock* with a wire.
The electrical wires *smoked* when they became hot.
*She was blinking* because she had tears in her eyes.
*He closed his eyes* when he was blinded by a bright light.
A lion *attacked* the lion tamer.
*When she takes off her glasses‚* she doesn’t see well.

Syllabary: ᎢᎦᏓ ᎠᎦᎵᏍᎬ ᏯᏁᏙᎭ ᏧᎵᏏᎩ ᏗᎦᏘᏅᏘᏗ ᏓᎾᎦᏘᏅᏛᏍᎪᎢ.
Transliteration: igada agalisgv yanedoha julsihgi diktinvtdi danaktinvtvsgoi
English: When some people are in the sun‚ *they put on* dark *glasses.*
Tone: igá:da à:gá:li:sgv̋ yané:dő:ha jű:lsihgi di:ktinv̋:tdi dà:naktinv:tvsgó:’i

Syllabary: ᏑᏟᎶᏓ ᎢᎦ ᏎᎷ ᏚᎦᏢᎲ ᏐᏈᎵ ᎤᎩᏍᏗ.
Transliteration: suhliloda iga selu duktlvhv sogwili jugisdi.
English: *He shelled* a bushel of *corn* for the horse to eat.
Tone: su:hli:lő:da i:ga̋: sě:lú dù:ktlv:hv́ so:gwíli jű:gì:sdi.

English: Who *was snoring* last night?
*She is sprinkling* water on the clothes she is going to iron.
Do you like *turnips?*
A cat caught a *woodpecker.*
Bill wants *to put on* some new *shoes.*
*He changed shoes* because they were too long.
*Take your* right *shoe off.*
If he can’t get up‚ *help him up.*
*He’s recuperating* in a hospital.
When I say *stand up*‚ everyone *stand up.*
*Tahlequah* is the capitol of the Cherokee Nation of Oklahoma.
*I am going to take my gloves off.* What about you?
*I am putting on gloves* because I am going to work.
*He took off his* shoes and *socks.*
While he was *putting on his socks‚* someone stole his shoes.
*Change your* wet *socks.*
Don’t *aim at* anyone with a gun.
*A horsefly* bites hard.
*They will be having a trial* on Monday.

Syllabary: ᏥᏈᏍᏗ ᎠᏂᏣᎳᎩ ᏚᎾᏠᏒ ᏓᎵᏆ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎦᏬᏂᏍᎬ ᎤᎾᏛᎪᏗ ᎤᏂᏰᎸᏒᎢ.
Transliteration: tsigwisdi anijalagi dunadlosv daligwa ugvwiyuhi gawonisgv unahtvgodi uniyelvsvi.
English: A lot of Cherokees *met* at Tahlequah in order to hear the chief speak.
Tone: tsigwi̋:sdi ani:jalagi dù:nadlo:sv́ dalígwa ugvwiyuhí gawó:ni:sgv̋: u:nahtv:gò:di̋ ù:ni:ye:lv́:sv̌:’i.

English: Why did they *separate?*

Syllabary: ᎦᏙ ᎤᏍᏗ ᏓᏁᎶᏗ Ꮎ ᎤᏍᏗ ᎠᏧᏣ?
Transliteration: gado usdi danehldi na usdi achuja?
English: What is the little boy *playing with?*
Tone: gado usdi dà:né:hldi ná usdi̋: achű:ja?

English: I am *going to play* ball all day tomorrow.
Cherokees *hold church services* on Saturday nights.
What did you get for *Christmas?*
When *are they going to adjourn?*

Syllabary: ᏎᎦᏨ ᏚᏍᏓᏲᏢ ᎦᎶᏇ ᎠᏍᏓᏴ ᎪᏣᏁᏍᏗᎢ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ.
Transliteration: segajv dusdayohlv galogwe asdayv gojanesdii iyusdi.
English: *He* barely *shot* the gun because the trigger was hard to pull.
Tone: se:gà:jv̋: dù:sdayo:hlv́ galò:gwé à:sdâ:yv̋: go:jane:sdi̋:’i iyű:sdi.

English: *He’s splitting* some cook wood.
*I am sprinkling* water on my cucumbers.
The treasurer *is counting* money.
*He curses* when something makes him angry.
When the president finished his speech‚ the people *applauded.*
Don’t work so hard *or you’ll get exhausted.*
When it becomes light‚ the dog *barks.*

Syllabary: ᎤᎾᏙᏓᏉᏅ ᎤᏒ ᎤᏬᏂᎯᏍᏗ ᏚᏒᏍᏔᏅᎢ ᎬᏩᏔᏲᏎᎳ ᎤᏬᏂᎯᏍᏗᎢ.
Transliteration: unadodagwohnv usv uwonihisdi dusvstanvi gvwahtayosela uwonihisdii.
English: *He scheduled* his speech for Monday night when asked to speak.
Tone: u:nadǒ:dágwohnv̋: usv̋: uwò:ni̋:hisdi dù:sv:stánv́:’i gv:wahtayo:se̋:la uwò:nǐ:hísdi̋:’i.

English: What time did *you* start *ironing?*
He found an arrowhead made out of *flint.*
When it freezes‚ the roads get *slippery.*

Syllabary: ᎢᎦᏓ ᏓᏬᎵ ᎠᏃᏍᏓ ᏗᎦᏰᏍᏗ ᎢᎦᏓᏃ ᎠᎾᏓᎯᎯ.
Transliteration: igada dawoli anosda digahyesdi igadahno anadahihi.
English: Some *mushrooms* are good to eat but some are poisonous.
Tone: igá:da dawó:li anő:sda diga:hye̋:sdi i:gá:dahnó: anada̋:hihi.

English: He stopped the leak in the tub with *a cork.*
*Let* the rabbit go.
She picked *a sack* full of hickory nuts.
*He slammed* the door when he went out.

Syllabary: ᏅᏃᎯ ᏕᎦᎾᏗᏫᏍᎬ ᎠᏫ ᏥᎪᎥ ᎤᏒ ᏥᎨᏒᎢ.
Transliteration: nvnohi deganadiwsgv ahwi jigov usv jigesvi.
English: I saw a deer *crossing* a road last night.
Tone: nv:no:hi de:gánadî:wsgv̋ ahwi ji:go’v́ usv̋: jigè:sv̋:’i.

English: I want *to announce* something.
*I crossed* the ocean when I was in the military service.
*He endured* working in the heat all day.
The Sunday School teacher *explained* what they had read.
*He decided* too quickly.
She *washes* the *dishes* immediately after they finish eating.
She’s *shelling* peas.
How many hickory nuts *are you going to shell?*
Summer begins in the month of *June.*
When he went around the curve too fast‚ he *turned* the car *over.*
The singers *sang* all night.
The cat *clawed him* with its sharp claws.
What *are you* doing‚ *washing ?*
Where did *marriage* begin?
The *murderer *was taken to jail when he was caught.
The lawyer is going to the *courthouse.*
*A photographer* came to the ball game.
Whose *photograph* is this?
Some graves have *crosses.*
Do you know where *a conjurer* lives?
That man is a Cherokee *medicine man.*

Syllabary: Ꮎ ᎤᏍᏗ ᎠᏧᏣ ᏗᏓᏰᏠᎯ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗ ᎤᏚᎵ ᎠᏛᏒᎢ.
Transliteration: na usdi achuja didahyehlohi iyulsdohdi uduli atvsvi.
English: That little boy wants to become *a surgeon* when he grows up.
Tone: ná usdi̋ achű:ja didahye̋:hlohi iyú:lsdohdi̋ ù:du:lí à:tvsv̋:’i.

English: He went back *home* a while ago.
An old man is cleaning some *dentures.*
Some men are working in the *cemetery* mowing grass.
I want to talk with a *policeman.*
He chose five *cooks* to cook.
He went to a school for *barbers.*
Have you been in *jail?*
Where is the *leader* of your group?
They formed a *loan association* in a big city.
He became a deacon in a First *Baptist* Church.`

Syllabary: ᏐᏁᎳ ᏍᎪᎯ ᎢᏯᏂᎢ ᏗᎾᏕᏠᏆᏍᎩ ᏚᏂᏍᏆᏛ ᎪᎯ ᎪᎨᏱ.
Transliteration: sohnela sgohi iyanii dinadelhgwasgi dunisgwadv kohi gogeyi.
English: Ninety *students* graduated this spring.
Tone: sóhně:la sgǒ:hi iyani̋:’i di:nade̋:lhgwà:sgi dù:ni:sgwá’dv́ kò:hi go:ge̋:yi.

English: She’s been *a teacher* for a few years.
I saw him walking *toward* the road.
I couldn’t find the *meeting place.*
Don’t let *excuses* get in your way.
How much does a bushel of *pears* cost?

Syllabary: ᎯᎳ ᎢᎦ ᏕᎪᏢ ᏧᏂᎳᏫᏍᏗ ᏓᎵᏆ ᎦᏚᎲᎢ?
Transliteration: hila iga degohlv junilawisdi daligwa gaduhvi?
English: How many *churches* are there in the city of Tahlequah?
Tone: hilá: i:ga̋: de:gó:hlv́ ju:nilà:wisdi̋: dalígwa gadú:hv̋:’i?

Syllabary: ᏗᎦᎴᏅᎯᏓ ᎩᏟ ᏗᎦᎴᏅᎯᏓ ᎠᏂᎳᏛ ᎤᏭᏓᎸᏤᎸ ᎦᎴᏂ ᎦᏅᎯᏒ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ.
Transliteration: digalenvhida gihli digalenvhida asiladv uwudalvchelv galeni ganvhisv iyusdi.
English: The *long-eared* hound dog got his ear caught in the fence because his ear was so long.
Tone: digale:nv́:hi:da gi:hli digale:nv́:hi̋:da à:sǐ:lá’dv̋ ù:wu:dalv̂:che:lv́: ga’leni ganv:hí:sv̋: iyű:sdi.

Syllabary: ᏗᎦᎶᎩᏍᎩ ᎤᏫᏒᏅ ᎤᎧᏲᏐᏅ ᎾᎦᏍᎬᎾ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ.
Transliteration: digalogisgi uhwisvnv ukayosohnv nagasgvna gesv iyusdi.
English: The *farmer’s* crop became dry because of no rain.
Tone: digalo:gi̋:sgi ù:hwisv́:nv̋: ù:kayo:sohnv́: nà:gǎ:sgv̋:na gè:sv̋: iyű:sdi.

English: Do you think there are enough *dumplings?*
Does anyone have an *announcement?*
She put the pies in the *pantry.*
He’s ticklish in *his ribs.*
I have bread in the *oven.*
Some people use *gigs* whenever they hunt crawdads.
I am reading *a book.*
While they were wrestling‚ he was accidentally stabbed with a *pencil.*
He asked for a *discussion* when he read the Bible.
I just saw *a mud dauber* sting a spider.

Syllabary: ᏦᏍᎪ ᏧᏒᎯᏓ ᏧᎾᏓᏍᏚᏗ ᎤᏴᏍᏗ ᏚᏭᎪᏓᏁᎸ ᏗᎫᎪᏗᏍᎩ.
Transliteration: josgo jusvhida junadasdudi uyvsdi duwukdanelv digukdisgi
English: The *judge* sentenced him to thirty days in jail.
Tone: jo’sgǒ: ju:sv̋:hida ju:nada:sdù:di̋ u:yv̋:sdi dù:wu:kdâ:ně:lv́ digu:kdi̋:sgi

English: He has a dog that’s a snake *killer.*
Do you hear *a telephone* ringing?
He wants to become a high *jumper.*
The wealthy have expensive *clothes.*
How many *covers* do you want?
I was *a wrestler* when I was in the twelfth grade.
*A carpenter* is buying some tools.
He has *spurs* on his boots.

Syllabary: ᏂᎦᏚ ᏯᏆᏕᏘᏴᏓ ᏥᎨᏒ ᎠᏆᏕᏠᏆᎥ ᏗᏣᎳᎩ ᏗᎩᎪᎵᏰᏗ.
Transliteration: nigadu yagwadetiyvd jigesv agwadehlohgwav dijalagi digigoliyedi.
English: I learned to read *Cherokee* when I was fourteen years old.
Tone: nigádu yágwade:tiyv̋:d jigè:sv̋: à:gwade:hlohgwá’v́ di:jalagi di:gigo:lǐ:yê:di̋:.

English: He went toward the *east.*
They use *musical instruments* when they sing.
Do *angels* have wings?
When some people break the *law*‚ they get away with it.
The *firemen* have three new *firetrucks.*
Hand me *a sharpener.* I’m going to sharpen a knife.
This man is *blind.*
He had to get *eyeglasses.*
If you look at him with *binoculars‚* you’ll recognize him.
He lost a new deck of *cards.*
I put *pepper* on my eggs.
She likes to use sharp *scissors* while sewing.
Are these boys *brothers?*
*His brother* is coming in a little while.
Did you get the *toy* you wanted?
Why did they become *enemies?*
When he saw *his enemy* coming‚ he ran away.
Help me move *a dresser.*
Do you know your *numbers?*
I want three gallons of *paint.*
I am looking for an electric *drill.*
The *iron* isn’t heating up.
When did he become *a lawyer?*
Someone is gathering *poke.*
*Bats* live in caves.
How big do *leeches* get?

Syllabary: ᏜᏱᎬ‚ ᎣᎦᎳᎰᎻ‚ ᎦᏚᎲ ᏥᎩ ᏜᏱᎦ ᏚᏙᏍᏔᏁᎢ.
Transliteration: dlayhgv oklahoma gaduhv jigi dlayhga dudostanei.
English: The town of Jay‚ Oklahoma‚ was named after the *bluejay.*
Tone: dlà:yhgv̋: o:klaho:ma ga:dú:hv̋: ji̋gi dlà:yhga dù:dó:stané:’i.

English: He’s in jail at *Jay.*
He works all day in the *field* plowing.
A little boy made a bird house for *martins.*
I will get there *about* ten o’clock.
We met *somewhere* down the road.
Let’s go swimming *Sunday* afternoon.
How early do you get up on *Saturday* mornings?
He’s returning a week from *Monday.*
It’s *really* cold outside.
Ouch! A *mosquito* bit me.
*Ants* are always working.
He came from *outside.*
It’s *really* cold outside‚ isn’t it?
He took *his family* to a ball game.

Syllabary: Ꮭ ᎩᎶ ᏳᎸᏉᏗ ᎤᏲ ᎠᏓᏅᏓᏗᏍᏗ ᏧᏠᎯᏍᏗᎢ.
Transliteration: hla kilo yulvkwdi uyo adanhdadisdi judlohisdii
English: No one likes *to be confronted with* a bad feeling.
Tone: hla kilő yu:lv́:kwdî u:yő ada:nhdadì:sdi ju:dlo:hisdi̋:’i

English: My dog’s *name* is ’Chief.’
*He* tried hard not *to stagger.*
If he walks in the hot sun‚ *he gets a rash.*
*It had horns* four feet long.
Dust *makes me sneeze.*
I wonder what’s causing *him to vomit.*
When she’s out in the sun‚ *she wears* dark *glasses.*
A boy is walking around *without his shoes on.*

Syllabary: ᏚᎵᏍᏗ ᎧᎸ ᎠᏂᏣᎳᎩ ᏓᎵᏆ ᎤᏂᏍᏆᎸᎡᎰ ᎤᎾᏕᏘᏱᏍᎬᎢ.
Transliteration: dulisdi kalv anijalagi daligwa unisgwalveho unadetiyisgvi.
English: Cherokee Holiday is held at Tahlequah in the month of *September.*
Tone: du:lì:sdi ká’lv̋ ani:jalagi dalígwa ù:ni:sgwalv́’e:hó: u:nade:tiyi:sgv̋:i.

English: I don’t like *to wear gloves* when I’m working.
What color *socks is he wearing?*
I see a squirrel in the *fork* of a tree.
That student *works* all day Saturday.
He hasn’t been here since the month of *October.*
I hear someone *making footsteps.*
When he pulled the rope hard‚ *it slipped out of my hand.*
*Is he tired* when he returns from work?
Why *did you let go* of the rope?
I am telling you the *truth.*
They *are walking around* barefoot.
Is your family *here?*
*I am going* far away.
They’re building a *large* house.
The soldiers crossed the *sea* in a ship.
*He lived* in Tahlequah for five years.
Where are those ball players *from?*
Indians are *Indigenous *to America.

Syllabary: ᎡᏝᏪ ᎨᏒ ᏴᏫ ᎦᏬᏂᏍᎩ ᎦᏬᏂᏍᎬ ᎢᎪᎯᏓ.
Transliteration: ehlawe gesv yvwi gawonisgi gawonisgv igohida.
English: The people were *quiet* while the speaker spoke.
Tone: e:hlawe̋: gè:sv́ yv:wi gawò:ni̋:sgi gawó:ni:sgv̋ i:go:hi̋:da.

English: He’s watching *animals* on television.
He sat *down.*
Did you come on *foot?*
They’re coming back shortly‚ *I think.*
That’s *enough!*
*Can* you take me to town?
*It seems like* he would say where he is going.
If there was a *straight* road‚ it would have been all right.

Syllabary: ᎠᎹ ᎤᎶᎯᏍᏗ ᎤᏒᏙᎾ ᎤᏥᏈᏛᏁᎸ ᎠᏎᏅ ᎤᏩᏥᏃᏌᏅᎢ.
Transliteration: ama ulohisdi usvdona utsgwidvnelv asehno uwachinosahnvi
English: The water pipe bent‚ but *he straightened it* out.
Tone: ama u:lo:hisdi u:sv:dő:na u:tsgwidv̂:ne:lv́ a:sé:hnó ù:wa:chinosahnv̌:’i

Syllabary: ᎦᏓᎭ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎢᏕᎭ; ᎭᏢᏊ ᏳᏫᎩᎷᏣ ᎦᏓ ᎢᏗᎪᏩᏘᏍᎪᎢ.
Transliteration: gadaha elohi ideha; hadlvgwu yuwigiluhja gada idigohwahtisgoi.
English: We live in a dirty world; no matter where we go we see *dirt.*
Tone: ga:da:ha̋ e:lo:hi ì:dě:ha; ha:dlv̋:gwu yuwì:gi̋:luhja gǎ:da ì:di:gohwahti:sgó:’i.

English: What’s that *hanging * on the post?
How old is that *flag?*
Did you make this *crock?*
He has a pipe made of *clay.*

Syllabary: Ꮭ ᏳᏚᎸᎮ ᎦᏓᎭ ᏓᏆᎴᎳ ᎤᎯᎸᏍᏗ ᎥᏍᎩᏅ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎤᏅᎦᎸᎲᎢ.
Transliteration: hla yudulvhe gadaha dagwalela uhilvsdi nasgina iyusdi unvgalvhvi.
English: He didn’t want to drive a *dirty* car‚ so he washed it.
Tone: hlá yu:dú:lv:hé: ga:da:ha̋ dagwalé:lâ u:hílv̀:sdi̋ nà:sginá iyű:sdi ù:nv:galv:hv̌:’î.

Syllabary: ᎪᎯᎩᏴ ᏥᎨᏎ ᏗᏂᎶᎩᏍᎩ ᏐᏈᎵ ᏓᏅᏗᏍᎨ ᏓᏂᏓᎷᎩᏍᎬ ᏠᎨᏏ.
Transliteration: gohigiyv jigese dinilogisgi sogwili danhdisge danidalugisgv dlogesi.
English: Long ago the farmers used horses for *plowing* the fields.
Tone: go:hi:giyv̋: jigè:se̋ di:ni:lo:gi̋:sgi so:gwíli dà:nhdi:sgê: dà:ni:dalu:gî:sgv̋: dlo:ge̋:si.

English: Have you used a horse-drawn *plow?*
He *picked *a ripe apple to eat.
*Take* the shirt *down* from the line.
*Turn it* to the left.

Syllabary: ᎦᏗᎨᏂ ᎤᏰᏝᎸ ᎫᎫ ᎤᏲᏨ ᎤᎳᏍᏛᏔᎾ ᎠᎹᏱ ᎠᏚᎯᏂᏙᎲᎢ.
Transliteration: gadigeni uhyehlalv gugu uyojv ulasdvtana amayi aduhinidohvi.
English: A broken glass cut *his heel* when he stepped on it while wading in water.
Tone: ga:di:ge̋:ni ù:hyehlálv́ gù:gu u:yò:jv̋ ù:lâ:sdv̋:tana amǎ:yi à:du:hi:nǐ:dô:hv̋:’i.

English: Can *you spin* the wheel?
*He strapped* the box with some leather.

Syllabary: ᎦᏙ ᎭᏛᏁ? Ꮭ ᎤᏟ ᎢᎦ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ. ᏗᎪᏪᎵᏊ ᏕᏥᎪᎵᏰᎠ.
Transliteration: gado advne hla udli iga gohusdi digohweligwu dejigoliyea.
English: *What* are you doing? Nothing much. I’m just reading a book.
Tone: gadò: adv́:ne hla udli̋ iga̋ go:hű:sdi digo:hwe:ligwu de:jígo:lǐ:yé’a.

English: *What’s* wrong with you? I’m sleepy.
The police *were standing* outside when I got there.
Children like to play *on the ground.*
*Why* didn’t you come when I was calling you?
I just *feel sorry for him.*
When he got tired of holding the coat‚ *he dropped it.*
The shirt *fell from* the line.
*Which* color are you going to paint it?
*What* do you see? A green pencil.
*What* do you want? I want you to help me.
The mirror is *on top* of the dresser.
Long ago‚ Cherokees helped each other through *cooperative labor.*

Syllabary: ᎯᎳ ᎾᏂᎠ ᏴᏫ ᎠᏁᎭ ᏓᎵᏆ ᎦᏚᎲᎢ?
Transliteration: hila nania yvwi aneha daligwa gaduhvi
English: What’s the population of the *town* of Tahlequah?
Tone: hilá: nà:ní:’a yv:wi à:ne:ha dalígwa ga:dú:hv̋:’i

English: She’s getting ready *to bake* a cake.
When the photos got *wet*‚ they were ruined.
Forest rangers build lookout towers on *hills*.

Syllabary: ᎦᏚ ᎤᎦᎾᏍᏓ ᎤᏩᏚᏅ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎠᏧᏣ ᎤᏚᏘᏱᏍᎬᎢ.
Transliteration: gadu uganasda uwaduhnv uweji achuja udetiyisgvi.
English: She baked a *cake* for her son’s birthday.
Tone: gá:du u:gana̋:sda ù:wá:du:hnv́ uwě:ji achű:ja ù:de:tiyi:sgv̋:’i.

English: *I am hanging* my shirt on the line.
This boy ate five of the *cucumbers.*
He borrowed my *paddle* yesterday.
Three of them are taking turns *rowing* the boat.
The reason he is so strong is because he is always lifting *heavy* objects.
*Who* is going to drive the car?

Syllabary: ᎦᎪ Ꮎ ᏌᎪᏂᎨ ᎤᎵᏍᏚᎵ ᏥᎩ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ?
Transliteration: gago na sahkonige ulsduli jigi asgaya?
English: *Who* is the man wearing the blue cap?
Tone: gá:go ná sahkǒ:níge̋ ù:lsdű:li ji̋gi asgaya?

English: The *wheel* went into the water when it came off.
A woodpecker *is pecking* on a pole.
He used *wedges* in splitting posts.

Syllabary: ᎦᏙ ᎤᏍᏗ ᎦᎵᏗ ᏔᎷᎩᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ?
Transliteration: gado usdi gahldi talugisgi na asgaya?
English: What is the man *putting* in the can?
Tone: gado u:sdi gahldi talu:gì:sgi ná asgaya?

English: Hand me the sugar *bowl.*
*Seven* days make one week.
You be the *seventh.*
When he broke *an arrow*‚ he went and got another one.
Early at night he’s already *asleep.*
*She’s gathering* hickory nuts to make kanuchi.

Syllabary: ᎦᏙ ᎤᏍᏗ ᎠᏓ ᏭᏗᏍᎪ ᎦᎵᏣᏗ ᏱᏦᏢᎿ?
Transliteration: gado usdi ada hvhdisgo gahljadi yijohlvhna?
English: What type of wood do you use when you make *a bow?*
Tone: gado ű:sdi ada hvhdi:sgó: gahlja’di yijohlv̋:hna?

English: I am going to build *a house.*
He’s buying some metal *roofing.*
When he goes fishing‚ he lives in *a tent.*
He *piled* the gravel on the road.
I *tied up* the prisoner with a rope.
The workers *are putting* some rocks in a hole in the ground.
The children *were getting sleepy*; that’s why we came back.
*Take* a coat‚ because it’s cold.
*I am holding* a new shirt.
*He’s handling* a dead snake.
The *collar* on the shirt just came off.
He made an extremely beautiful *saddle.*
What is the rabbit *eating?*
Green beans are good *to eat.*
*He ripped* an old cloth to wash the car with.

Syllabary: ᎦᎵᏦᏕ ᎤᏴᏍᏗ ᏳᏚᎵ ᏪᏌ‚ ᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗ ᏕᎦᏣᎵᎪᏍᎪᎢ.
Transliteration: galhjode uyvsdi yuduli wesa galohisdi degajalgosgoi
English: When the cat wants to go inside the house‚ *he scratches* on the door.
Tone: galhjo:dé u:yv:sdi̋ yu:dúli̋ wě:sa galo:hisdi̋: de:gá:jalgo:sgó:’i

English: He has a *fast* car.
When I had a cold‚ a doctor *gave me an injection.*
I guess she’s just about finished washing *because she is wringing* the clothes.
What is the wasp *stinging?*
I almost brought my dog‚ but *I left it behind.*
*I am giving him* one cat.
He bought ten bundles of *young* onion *plants.*
*I am wrapping* a Christmas gift.
*He put* the knife *in* his pocket.
He bought a new tool *box.*
Which *street* do you live on?
*He took* a half dollar out of his pocket.
*She took* some clothes out of a basket.

Syllabary: ᏪᏌ ᎤᎨᎮᎾ ᎩᏟ‚ ᏪᏌ ᏡᎬᎢ ᎤᎸᏒᎢ.
Transliteration: wesa ukehena gihli wesa dluhgvi ulvsvi
English: When the dog chased the cat‚ the cat *climbed* a tree.
Tone: wě:sa ù:ké:he̋:na gi:hli wě:sa dluhgv̋:’i ù:lv́:sv̌:’i

English: A *blacksnake* likes to eat eggs.
They pierced *their ears* to wear earrings.
When he placed his hand on the stove‚ *he was burned.*
When the wind blew hard‚ the tree *fell.*
The children *are climbing around* on an oak tree.
The forest *burned* for three days.
He’s in the garden *picking* corn.
When a dog was run over by a car‚ *it died.*

Syllabary: Ꮎ ᎦᎵᏦᎯᏓ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎠᏁᎵᏗ ᎤᏓᎨᏒ ᎤᏲᎱᏎᏗᎢ.
Transliteration: na galjohida asgaya anelhdi udagesv uyohusehdii.
English: The *fat* man is trying to lose weight.
Tone: ná galjő:hida asgaya à:né:lhdi u:da:gé:sv̋ u:yo:hu:sehdi̋:’i.

English: He *spanked* his son.
He *hoes* his garden every day.
Where did you put the *hoe?*
He’s cleaning a *gun.*
My neighbor *stopped by* on his way to the store.
He went through the *doorway.*
He tried to *overtake me*‚ but I was walking too fast.
His birthday is in *August.*
Whenever someone is not alert‚ *he tricks him.*

Syllabary: ᏙᏓᏥᎶᏁᎢᏒ ᎦᏰᏫᏍᎩ ᎠᏎᏅ ᎦᏳᎳ ᏧᎶᏁᏓ ᎨᏒᎢ.
Transliteration: dodajiloneisv gayewsgi asehno gayula juloneda gesvi.
English: *I was going to oil* the sewing machine‚ but she had already *oiled it.*
Tone: do:dajílo:né’ì:sv́ ga:yě:wsgí a:sé:hnó: gá:yu:la ju:lo:ne̋:da gè:sv̌:’i.

English: *Knock* the bug *off* the potato.
Whenever he thinks he can get away with it‚ *he cheats.*
*He passed by us* without even saying anything.
*He passed* easily when he was tested.
*He* almost *fell.*
*He arrives* at five o’clock.
Why didn’t they start? *Was* there something *missing?*
He used *an axe* to fell the tree.

Syllabary: ᏧᏓᏅᎦᎸᏓ ᏂᏙᏩᏅᎾ ᏱᎩ ᏗᏤᏍᏙ ᎦᎷᏯᏍᏗ ᏯᎾᎩᎸᎥᎦ.
Transliteration: judanvgalvda nidowahnvna yigi dijesdo galuysdi yanhkilvvga.
English: If you don’t keep the mattresses clean‚ *bed bugs* will get on them.
Tone: ju:danv:galv̋:da nidowâ:hnv̋:na yi̋gi di:je:sdó galù:ysdi yanhkilv’vga.

English: *He’s chopping* on a big tree.
Where is the laundry *basket?*
*Did you beat him up* when you two fought?
Is *heaven* high in the sky?
The airplane is flying *high.*
The ball flew by *above* your head.
Climb up *higher.*
The *sky* is blue.
*He put* the dead snake in a hole in the ground.
Hurry! Let’s get there *before he sells* the house.
He has a lot of *livestock* on his place.
*I’m hiring him* for as long as he wants to work.
Who is that *lazy* man?
This is a *gelding.*
Where *did you find* the belt?
*Are you going to take* a coat?
He has a *dangerous* job.
Where *did you get* your cap?
*I am changing* the wheel.
He has a shirt made of deer *skin.*
*Take* three bats to the baseball diamond.
He likes cucumbers so well‚ he eats them *without peeling them.*
*She got* a gallon of milk.
*He’s milking* a cow.
Did you find out who was *bewitching you?*
*He is holding* a 20 gauge shotgun.
How much milk *did you leave?*
*I just gave him* water to drink.
The dog is trying *to find* some water.
*He took* some water to the workers.
A ball hit him on *his chest.*
*He lives* in a new house.
Is your friend *married?*
The roads *are freezing.*

Syllabary: ᏑᏕᏘᏴᏓ ᏥᎨᏒ ᎪᎯ ᎢᎦ ᏍᏓᏯ ᎤᏁᏐᏯᏒᎢ.
Transliteration: sudetiyvda jigesv kohi iga sdaya unesoysvi
English: One year ago today *it hailed *hard.
Tone: su:de:tiyv̋:da jigè:sv̋: kò:hi i:ga sda̋:ya u:ne:sô:ysv̌:’i

English: A carpenter always uses a *saw* when building houses.
He always brushes *his teeth* after every meal.
*I’ll* just *lie* on the floor.
He burned *his tongue* drinking hot coffee.
When he stepped into the swimming pool‚ *he agitated* the water.
*I am taking* some drinking water for the workers.
*I have* five gallons of milk.
*He’s handling* some poison.
Where did the *salesman* go?
A *bullet* hit him in his shoulder.
The dog is trying to catch *his tail.*
Who *had* the bat last?
I don’t *hate* anyone.
Where shall I *set up* the bed?
She has a *bedstead* that belonged to her mother.
The man is going to buy *a bed.*
When the chief spoke‚ *he urged* the people to work together.
*He buried* a dead snake.
*He left* his shirt when he escaped from jail.
*Catch* the dog.
When was *he arrested?*
He wants to become *a catcher.*
*He hunts* all the time in the winter.
*A hunter* is going across a field.
An airplane just *flew by.*
Sometimes Spring Creek *floods.*
When he rang the bell‚ church services started.
He wants some *leather* to make belts.
Cool *wind blows* in the evening.
He’s in jail for *stealing* a car.
I don’t want to buy anything that has been *stolen.*
"A thief" was caught stealing.
The boat is *sinking.*
When *will he be buried?*
Can *you pull* a car *for me?*
Throw that *stick* away.
*Let me drag it* for awhile.
When it got hot inside‚ *I went out.*
We get our drinking water from *a spring.*
Can you tell me where the *exit* is?
He is mowing the *grass.*
He’s repairing a *lawn mower.*
The strong man *is twisting* steel.

Syllabary: Ꮲ ᏍᎪᎯ ᎢᏳᏩᎩᏗ ᏂᏚᏍᏓᏲᏢ ᎠᏎᏅ ᏚᏅᏗᏏᎶᎥ ᎠᎪᏂᏍᎬᎢ.

English: He fired about ten times‚ but *he missed* the target *over and over.*
Tone: dlv̋: sgǒ:hi iyuwa̋:kdi nidù:sdayo:hlv́ a:sé:hnv́ dù:nv:dî:siló:’v́ agoní:sgv̋:’i.

English: She’s feeding a baby on *her breast.*
*He fell* hard playing ball.
*I’m cleaning* my fingernails.
*He’s hammering* on a nail.
*A hammer* is a carpenter’s tool.
They fix horseshoes at *a blacksmith shop.*
Mountain climbers carry *long* ropes.
When he roped the calf‚ *he tied up its legs.*
*Put your brakes on!*
Pull the *brake.*
He ran out of *brakes.*

Syllabary: ᏦᏍᎪ ᏦᎢ ᎪᏪᎵ ᎦᏅᏅ ᎾᎥ ᏥᏁᎳ.
Transliteration: josgo joi gohweli ganvhnv nav jinela.
English: I live near *highway* 33.
Tone: josgǒ: jo’i go:hwe̋:li ganv:hnv̋ na’v̋: jině:la.

English: *He tossed about* all night.
*I gave him* a new shirt.

Syllabary: ᏎᎷ ᎤᏓ ᎪᏢᏔᏅ ᎦᏅᏃᏩ ᎪᎦᏍᏗᏍᎩ ᎨᏒᎢ ᎡᏚᏚ.
Transliteration: selu uda gohlvhtanv ganvnowa goksdisgi gesvi edudu.
English: My grandfather used to smoke a corncob *pipe.*
Tone: sě:lú u:da go:hlvhtanv̋: ganv:no:wa go:ksdi̋:sgi gè:sv̌:’i e:dù:du.

English: He’s eating a turkey *leg.*

Syllabary: ᎦᏅᏍᎨᏂ ᎠᏥᏲᎴ ᏓᎿᏬᎢ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏥᏅᏍᏕᏍᏗ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁᎢ.
Transliteration: ganvsgeni ajiyole dahnawoi ale ajinvsdesdi nulstanei.
English: He was shot in *his leg* in a war‚ and it had to be amputated.
Tone: ganv:sge̋:ni à:ji:yo:lé da:hnawő:’i alé ajinv:sde̋:sdi nù:lstané:’i.

English: *His shoulder* hurts from throwing the ball hard.

Syllabary: ᏑᏟᎶᏓ ᎢᏳᏓᏅᎯᏓ ᎤᏩᏌᏙᏴ ᏓᏆᎴᎳ ᎩᎳ ᎤᏂᎩᏒᎢ.
Transliteration: suhliloda iyudanvhida uwasadohyv dagwalela kila uhnigisvi.
English: *He pushed* the car for a mile before it started.
Tone: su:hli:lő:da iyú:danv:hi̋:da uwa:sadô:hyv́ dagwalé:la kilá ù:hnigî:sv̌:’i.

Syllabary: ᏄᏝᏂᎬᎬ ᎬᏗᏍᎪ ᏳᏩᏌᏆᎴᏝ Ꮎ ᎤᏔᎾ ᎦᏆᏘᏗ.
Transliteration: nuhlanigvgv kdisgo yuwasagwalehla na utana gagwatdi.
English: He uses all his strength *when he rolls* the big wheel.
Tone: nuhlanǐ:gv̂:gv̋ kdi:sgô: yuwá:sagwale̋:hla ná ű:tana ga̋:gwatdi.

English: They have a *round* dinner table at home.
Put on *a coat* when you go outside.

Syllabary: ᎦᏌᎾᎵ ᏓᏳᎴᏅᎲ ᎠᏓᏪᎳᎩᏍᎬ Ꮎ ᎦᎵᏦᏕ ᏧᎪᏅᎢ.
Transliteration: gasanali dayulenvhv adawelagisgv na gahljode jugohnvi.
English: The fire started at the *roof* when the house burned.
Tone: ga:sana:lí dayu:lé:nv:hv́ à:dawě:lágì:sgv̋ ná gahljǒ:dé jù:gohnv̋:’i.

English: *She’s threading *a needle.
The old car *left a trail* of smoke as it took off.
Set up twenty *chairs.*
She’s looking for a new *dining table.*
My uncle made *a rocking chair.*
That cat is always sleeping *on a chair.*

Syllabary: ᎠᎹ ᎦᏍᏉᎢᎭ Ꮎ ᎦᏚᎵᏓ ᎠᎿᏬ ᏥᎦᏓ ᎠᏏᎳᏛᎢ.
Transliteration: ama gasgwoiha na gadulida ahnawo jigada asiladvi.
English: Water *is dripping* from the wet cloth hanging on a line.
Tone: ama gá:sgwo’iha ná ga:du:li̋:da ahnawó jigá’da à:sǐ:lá’dv̋:’i.

Syllabary: ᎦᏥᏃᏍᏓ ᎾᎵᏍᏛᏡᏍᎬᎾ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎦᏐᏆᏘ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅᎢ.
Transliteration: gachinosda nalsdvhlusgvna gesv iyusdi gasohgwati nulstanvi.
English: *He* became *humpbacked* because he wouldn’t sit up straight.
Tone: ga:chinő:sda nálsdv:hlusgv̋:na gè:sv̋ iyű:sdi gá:sohgwati nù:lstánv́:’i.

English: The man’s *back* hurts.
When the dog heard someone *whistling*‚ he took off running.
*He’s piling up* rocks he picked up in the field.
An electric light is hanging from a pole.
He lost a button from his shirt.
The cook is looking for *a cooking pan.*
*He was stabbed over and over* with a knife.
They prohibit drinking at the *stompgrounds.*
There is *a* squirrel *tail* hanging on a pole.
*She’s hanging up* a picture.
*He* really likes *to joke.*

Syllabary: ᎢᎾ ᏱᏚᎯᎴᎾ ᏩᎦ ᎯᎸᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏩᎩᏗ ᏕᎦᏪᏐᎸᏍᏗᏍᎪᎢ.
Transliteration: ina yiduhilena wahga hilvsgi iyuwakdi degawesolvsdisgoi
English: When he drives cattle for a long distance‚ *he rests them* a few times.
Tone: i̋:na yidu:hí’le̋:na wahga hilv̋:sgi iyuwa̋:kdi de:gáwe:so:lv:sdǐ:sgó:’i

English: If the dog has fleas‚ *he bathes it.*
*He baptized* ten people.
When you’re asked a question‚ *answer.*

Syllabary: ᎤᏁᎫᏨ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎠᏧᏣ ᏂᎪᎯᎸ ᎦᏬᏅᏗᏍᎪᎢ.
Transliteration: unegujv iyusdi uweji achuja nigohilv gawonhdisgoi
English: Because his son is so mean‚ *he* always *lectures* him.
Tone: u:ne:gú:jv̋: iyű:sdi uwě:ji achű:ja nigo:hí:lv̋ gawó:nhdǐ:sgó:’i

English: Don’t be listening to *gossip.*

Syllabary: ᏥᏈᏍᏗ ᎦᏬᏂᎭ ᎠᏎᏃ Ꮭ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏯᏗᎭ.
Transliteration: tsgwisdi gawoniha asehno hla gohusdi yadiha.
English: *He’s talking* a lot‚ but he’s not saying anything.
Tone: tsgwi̋:sdi gawó:ni:ha a:sé:hnó: hla go:hű:sdi yadí:ha.

English: The man raises large *cantaloupes.*
A wasp stung him on *his finger.*
*She’s sewing* a dress with a sewing machine.
She wants an electric *sewing machine.*

Syllabary: ᎩᎶ ᎦᏰᎪᎩ ᏱᎩ‚ ᏧᎶᏘᏍᎩ ᎦᏅᏓᏓ‚ ᎠᎾᏗᏍᎪᎢ.
Transliteration: kilo gayhgogi yigi julotsgi ganhdada anadisgoi.
English: If someone is *a liar*‚ they say he has a forked tongue.
Tone: kilő: gayhgő:gi yi̋gi jú:lő:tsgi ga:nhda’da à:nadǐ:sgó:’i.

English: He goes around telling *lies.*
Pour me *a little* coffee.
Have you *already* been to town?
The dog is hungry. *Feed it.*
The dog *howls* when he hears the wolves.
When their team won‚ the ballplayers *yelled.*
Have you ever tried to rope a *wild* horse?
I saw him walking *downstream* while ago.
She has to go to the *clinic* tomorrow.
*He’s leading* a small dog *by a leash.*
How much are the *pies?*
When he digs holes in a rocky place‚ he uses a *crowbar.*
Who buys *posts?*
*Set the post *here in the center.
There’s *blood* on your shirt.
When a ball hit him on his nose‚ *he got a nosebleed.*
*Red* is my favorite color.
There’s some *loose hair* on your shirt.
I see *a* mean *dog.*
I hear *a hounddog* barking.
This is how it’s written in *English.*
Lend me *a quarter.*
She’s going to quit teaching next *spring.*
I can’t wait till *summer.*
Did he take *a long time* building the house?
*He holds his* son’s *hand* when they cross the water.
*I agree with* what he says.
I want *you to vouch for me.*
He has a lot of *faith.*

Syllabary: ᎥᏍᏊ ᏍᎪᎯ ᎾᏕᏘᏯ ᎬᏩᎪᎲ ᏱᎩ ᎩᎶ‚ ᎠᎯᏓ ᎪᎵᎪᎢ.
Transliteration: vsgwu sgohi nadetiya gvwagohv yigi kilo ahida gohlgoi
English: Even if it’s been ten years since he has seen someone‚ *he recognizes him* easily.
Tone: v̀:sgwú sgǒ:hi nà:de:tǐ:ya gv:wago:hv̋ yi̋gi kilő ahi̋:da go:hlgó:’i

English: *Does he understand* Cherokee?
*Do you know* this man?
Pass me some *butter.*
If he eats just a little‚ *he gets full.*
What *are you making?*
*He accidentally dropped* the clock from the table.
The window pane *fell.*

Syllabary: ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎤᏂᏲ ᎠᏎᏅ Ꮭ ᏯᏆᏅᏔ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
Transliteration: gohusdi nihyo asehnv hla yagwanhta iyusdi gesvi.
English: They are looking for *something*‚ but I don’t know what it is.
Tone: go:hű:sdi :ni:hyó a:sé:hnv hla yagwá:nhta iyú:sdi gè:sv̋:’i.

English: They ordered some writing *tablets.*

Whose letter are you *writing?*

Syllabary: ᎤᏂᎪᏗ ᎠᏂᏣᎳᎩ ᏗᎾᏕᏠᏆᏍᎩ ᏓᎾᏕᏠᏆᏍᎪ ᎪᎢ ᏧᎾᏕᏠᏆᏍᏗᎢ
Transliteration: unigodi anijalagi dinadehlohgwasgi danadehlohgwasgo goi junadehlohgwasdii
English: A lot of Cherokee students go to school at *Greasy* School.
Tone: uni̋:gò:di ani:jalagi di:nade̋:hlohgwà:sgi dà:nade:hlohgwâ:sgó go’i̋ junadehlohgwasdi̋:’i

English: Sausage makes a lot of *grease.*
