Which finger do you use *when you pull the trigger?*
He’s oiling the *trigger* so it won’t rust.
There are twenty *cigarettes* in one package.
*He smokes* two packages of cigarettes in one day.
Do you like *winter?*
The doctor has *pneumonia.*

Syllabary: ᎠᏥᎳ ᎤᏥᎦᏘ ᎬᏗ ᎠᎩᏢᎭ ᎦᎷᏯᏍᏗ‚ ᎪᏍᏓᏯ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅᎢ.
Transliteration: ajila utsgati gvhdi akdlvha galuysdi gosdaya nulstanvi.
English: When I sharpened the axe with a file‚ it became *sharp.*
Tone: ajǐ:la ű:tsgati gv̋hdi à:kdlv:ha galùysdi gő:sdà:ya nù:lstánv́’i.

English: He was using a *dull* axe to chop wood.
They lost the ball *when he batted.*
How many *bats* did you bring?
*Tell him* I said hello when you see him.
His leg *swelled* where a snake bit him.
Let’s sit by the *fire.*
Last night *I built* a big *fire.*
*Unhitch* the horse and let him graze.
*He unhooked* the chain when the car started.
*he unplugs* the television when it’s lightning.
*He hitched up* his horse at the hitching post.
Did *he hook up* the battery?
These*bottles* contain milk.
*Ticks* are making the dog itch.
Have you been to *Bartlesville?*
He’s whittling *a handle* for an axe.
Who *put a handle on* this hoe?
*Quail* hunters sometimes accidentally shoot one another.
When she finishes peeling potatoes‚ *she washes them* in water.
*She’s accusing him* of stealing apples.
How many *lids* do you want?
*He covered* his scar with his hand.
*He is putting a lid on* the jar containing water.
*She boiled* three eggs for breakfast.
*Squirrels* eat acorns.
I see a *mourning dove* flying.
The *Muskogee* fair is in the month of October.

Syllabary: ᎦᏳᎳ ᎩᎶ ᏥᏅᏫᏍᏔᎾ ᏧᏭᏖᏍᏗ ᎯᎠ ᏅᏯ ᏣᏂᎳᎨᏯ.
Transliteration: gayula kilo jinvwstana juwutesdi hia nvya janilageya.
English: *I’ve already asked* someone to pick these rocks up.
Tone: gá:yu:la kilő ji:nv:wstana juwu:te:sdi̋ hi’a nv̀:ya jà:ni:lage̋:ya.

English: *It snowed* on Christmas day.
He raises *watermelons* every summer.
Mother *is sifting* flour.
The roof on the house *leaks* when it rains.
He hit the nail *with* a hammer.
She conversed *by means of * Cherokee.
I heard *a bobcat* last night.
They say *he is* barely *alive.*
He has a very *active* family.
I have a *black* car.
The batter *hit* the ball hard.
Someone *is knocking* on the door.
What *do you have cooking?*
Is that snake lying there *alive?*

Syllabary: Ꮎ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏢᎢ ᎬᏅᎢ ᎢᎪᎯᏓ ᎠᏥᏍᏚᏗ ᏚᏚᎪᏓᏏ.
Transliteration: na asgaya yvwi uhlvi kvhnvi igohida ajisdudi dudukdasi.
English: The *murderer* was sentenced to life imprisonment.
Tone: ná asgaya yv:wi uhlv̋:’i kv:hnv̋:’i i:gó:hi̋:da à:jisdu:di dù:du:kdâ:si.

English: He got choked trying to swallow something *whole.*
He likes *fried* potatoes.
What *are you frying?*
How many *turkeys* do you own?
*When* the sunshine *melted* the snow‚ it became muddy.

Syllabary: ᎬᎾᏬᏍᎩ ᎪᏢᏔᏅ ᎤᎸᏌᏗ ᎤᏩᎾᏬᏒ ᎤᏗᏞᎩ ᎠᎹ ᎠᏟᏍᏛ ᏭᏝᎾ.
Transliteration: gvnawosgi gohlvhtanv ulvsadi uwanawosv udihlehgi ama adlisdv wuhlana.
English: A glass made of plastic *melted* when she put in some hot water.
Tone: gv:nawő:sgi gohlvhtanv̋: ulv̋:sa’di uwa:nawo:sv́: u:di̋:hlehgi ama à:dlî:sdv̋ wű:hlana.

English: Where is the *broom?*
*Sweep* the floor before you go.
When do blackberries *get ripe?*
*He is hiding* a stolen car.
Someone is coming. *Hide* the gun.
*He hid* his shirt in the drawer.
*He cracked* a bucket full of hickory nuts.
He doesn’t know how much coal *to put into the fire.*
How long *did you soak* the clothes?
Soldiers were *all around.*
*Where* did your friend go?

Syllabary: ᎡᎵᏊ ᏔᎵ ᎢᏳᏔᏬᏍᏔᏅ ᏱᎨᏓ ᎠᎹᏱ ᎭᏫᏂ.
Transliteration: eligwu tali iyutawostanv yigeda amayi hawini
English: I can stay *under* water for two minutes.
Tone: eli̋:gwu táli iyú:tawò:stánv̋ yige:da ama:yí hawi̋:ni

English: The cat is lying *underneath* the chair.
*Meat* is less expensive at slaughter houses.
*Don’t* be doing that.
*These* are my children.
*How much* does it cost?
*How long* do you want to use it?
*What* time is it?
*When* do you want me to come and visit you?
I have a total of *five* cows.
Are you hungry? *No.*
That’s *not* it.
What were you saying? *Nothing.*
I am going *nowhere* today.

Syllabary: ᎢᎦᏓ ᏴᏫ Ꮭ ᎢᎸᎯᏳ ᏧᎾᏓᏍᏚᏗ ᎤᏂᏴᏢ ᏱᎨᏐᎢ.
Transliteration: igada yvwi hla ilvhiyu junadasdudi uniyvhlv yigesoi.
English: Some people have *never* been in jail.
Tone: i:gá:da yv:wi hla ilv̌:híyű ju:nada:sdù:di̋ u:ni:yv:hlv́ yigè:ső:’i.

English: I see *no one* that I know.
He doctored himself where a wasp stung him by rubbing *mud* on it.
He’s washing a *muddy* car.
When it rains‚ the roads get *muddy.*
She fell *in the mud* when she slipped.
Come here *a while.*
Did he say he was coming *soon?*
Don’t be mean.
Cut down all the dead *trees.*
He’s feeding a mountain *lion.*
Let’s go swimming. *Okay.*
What are you going to use those *boards* for?

Syllabary: ᏯᏙᎵ ᏍᏚᏗ ᎬᏙᎠ ᏫᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗ ᏧᏪᏅᏒ ᏲᎦ.
Transliteration: hyahtoli sdudi ktoa wigalohisdi juwenvsv yoga.
English: You go through a *narrow* gate when you’re going to his house.
Tone: hyahtő:li sdù:di któ:’a wigaló:hisdi̋: juwé:nv̀:sv̋ yő:ga.

English: My mother is always sewing *quilts.*
How *many* do you want?
If it isn’t night‚ it’s *day.*
*Some* of the ball players didn’t come.
*He’s* just *returning* from fishing.

Syllabary: ᎯᎩᏍᎪ ᎢᎳᏏᏗ ᎢᎦᏅᎯᏓ ᏍᏕᏱᏓ ᎤᏮᏔᏅ ᏩᎦ ᎠᎩᎾ ᎠᏍᎳᏗᏍᎬᎢ.
Transliteration: hiksgo ilasihdi iganvhida sdeyida uwhtanv wahga agina asladisgvi.
English: He used a rope fifty feet *long* in roping a calf.
Tone: hiksgǒ: i̋:là:sihdi i:gánv:hi̋:da sde:yída ù:whtanv́ wahga agi̋:na à:sladǐ:sgv̋:’i.

English: I want to buy three *ricks* of wood.

Syllabary: ᏑᏓᎵ ᎢᎳᏏᏗ‚ ᏑᏓᎵ ᎢᏏᏔᏗᏍᏗ ᎢᎦᏘ Ꮎ ᎠᎳᏍᎦᎵᏍᎩ.
Transliteration: sudali ilasihdi sudali isihtadisdi igati na alasgalisgi.
English: That ballplayer is six feet‚ six inches *tall.*
Tone: sǔ:dáli i̋:là:sihdi sǔ:dáli i̋:sihtadì:sdi i̋:gati ná alà:sgali̋:sgi.

English: How *long* did it take?
I stayed there until they *adjourned.*
He has corn planted in an *area* of one hundred acres.
Did you get here *before* I did?
This man is the *first* speaker.
Let’s go pick some *green* beans.

Syllabary: ᎤᏪᏘ ᏓᏆᎴᎳ ᏧᎲ ᎠᏎᏃ ᏃᏊ ᎢᏤ ᎤᎭ.
Transliteration: uweti dagwalela juhv asehno nogwu ije uha.
English: He used to have an old car‚ but now he has a *new* one.
Tone: uwe̋:ti dagwalé:la jù:hv́ a:sé:hnó: nǒ:gwu ije̋: ù:ha.

English: A friend of mine lives in a *green* house.
How many years was he gone?
*Both* of you work tomorrow.
What *size* hat do you wear?
His boat is ten *feet* long.
Have you *ever* been bitten by a snake?

Syllabary: ᎢᎸᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏟᎶᏓ ᎡᎳᏓ ᎤᏂᎩᏒᎢ ᎩᎳ ᎤᏓᏣᏁᎴᎢ.
Transliteration: ilvsgi iyuhliloda elada uhnigisvi kila udajanelei.
English: He walked a *few* miles before he caught a ride.
Tone: ilv̋:sgi iyú:hli:lő:da e:lada ù:hnigî:sv̌:’i kilá u:da:jâ:ně:lé:’i.

English: When I was in the military service‚ I went *far* away.
Which *snake* is the most dangerous?
The lion went back into the *wilderness* when it escaped.
He’s *too far* away to hear you.
Go get some *flour* downtown.
Twelve *inches* make one foot.
Do you like pie made of *pumpkin?*
I want a fifteen-*cent* drink.

Syllabary: ᏦᎢ ᎢᏯᎦᏴᎵ ᎢᏯᏂ ᏴᏫ ᎤᎾᏛᏓᏍᏔᏂᏢ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎦᏬᏂᏍᎬᎢ.
Transliteration: joi iyagayvli iyani yvwi unahtvdastanihlv ugvwiyuhi gawonisgvi.
English: Three *thousand* people came to hear the President speak.
Tone: jo’i iyágayv̋:li iyáni̋: yv:wi ù:nahtv:dâ:stanǐ:hlv́ u:gv:wiyu:hí gawó:nisgv̋:’i.

English: It’s ten *minutes* past four.
How far *apart* do you plant your corn?
How *often* does he visit you?

Syllabary: ᏦᎢ ᎢᏳᏟᎶᏓ ᎢᏳᏓᏅᎯᏓ ᎤᏫᏛᎲ ᏔᎵ ᎢᏳᏟᎶᏓ ᎧᏄᎦᏟ.
Transliteration: joi iyuhliloda iyudanvhida uhwidvhv tali iyuhliloda kanugahli.
English: He carried two *gallons* of blackberries for three *miles.*
Tone: jo’i iyú:hli:lő:da iyú:danv:hi̋:da ù:hwi:dv:hv́ tá’li iyú:hli:lő:da kanu:gahli.

English: She used crutches for three *weeks.*
I want one *like* it.
He stopped by at twelve *o’clock.*
How many *times* have you gone swimming this summer?
Do you speak *Cherokee?*

Syllabary: ᎯᎳ ᎾᏂᎠ ᎠᏂᏣᎳᎩ ᏚᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁ ᏣᎳᎩ ᎠᏰᏟᎢ?
Transliteration: hila nania anijalagi dunilvhwisdane jalagi ayehlii?
English: How many *Cherokees* work at the Cherokee Nation?
Tone: hilá: nà:ní:’a ani:jalagi dù:nilv̌:hwísdà:ne jalagi aye:hli̋:’i?

English: There are tall mountains in *North Carolina.*

Syllabary: ᏥᎩᎵᎵ ᏱᏂᎦᏪ ᎠᏓᏁᎸ ᎾᎥ‚ ᎩᎶ ᏓᎦᎷᏥ ᎠᎾᏗᏍᎪ ᎠᏂᏣᎳᎩ.
Transliteration: jigilili yinigawe adanelv nav kilo dagaluhji anadisgo anijalagi.
English: When *a chickadee* sings near a house‚ Cherokees say that someone will come.
Tone: jigili:li yinigawe̋: à:da:né:lv̋ na’v̋: kilő: dagáluhji à:nadǐ:sgô ani:jalagi.

English: Get one package of *chewing gum*‚ okay?
Have you ever dreamed about *Jesus?*
My brother likes to hunt *rabbits.*
Let’s go hunt *crawdads.*
*A scorpion* stung him on his foot.

Syllabary: ᎢᎦᏓ ᏥᏍᏆ ᏧᎦᏃᏮ ᏩᏂᎶᏍᎪ ᎪᎳ ᏱᏄᎵᏍᏔᎾ.
Transliteration: igada jisgwa juganowv wanilosgo gola yinulstana.
English: Some *birds* migrate south in the winter.
Tone: i:gá:da jí:sgwa jugá:nowv̋: waní:lo:sgó gő:la yinű:lstana.

English: *A robin* has a red breast.
He likes to eat fried *chicken.*
*A rooster* is sitting on a post crowing.
I see an *airplane.*
Which man is the *pilot?*
Go *straight* ahead.
Let’s go *upstream.*
I’m going to Texas for *three* days.
Turn to the *third* page.
He’s been gone since *Wednesday.*
Long ago the Cherokees raised *tobacco.*
If the *windows* happen to be open‚ close them.
They have *various* things on sale.
Who is the *chairman* of your group?

Syllabary: ᏧᎦᎾᏮ ᏗᏜ ᏓᏄᎪᏗᏍᎪ ᏌᏌ ᎪᎳ ᏱᏄᎵᏍᏔᎾ.
Transliteration: juganawv didla danukdisgo sasa gola yinulstana.
English: Geese head *south* when winter comes.
Tone: ju:gá:nawv̋ dí:dla dà:nukdi:sgô: sa:sa gő:la yinű:lstana.

English: The farmer is planting *cabbage.*
Are you afraid of *centipedes?*

Syllabary: ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᏧᎬᏩᎶᏗ ᎢᎨᏎᏍᏗ Ꮭ ᏱᏛᎩᏩᏏ ᏓᏆᎴᎳ.
Transliteration: galvldi jugvwalhdi igesesdi hla yidvkiwasi dagwalela.
English: If the *price* is too high‚ I’m not going to buy the car.
Tone: galv̌:ldi ju:gv̋:walhdi igê:se̋:sdi hla yidv:kíwasi dagwalé:la.

English: *Wealth* does not cause happiness.
*In the past* there weren’t as many storms.

Syllabary: ᎪᎯᏗ ᎤᏗᏞᎬ ᏱᎦᏁ ᎤᏅᏗ ᏧᎿᏦᏍᏗ ᏂᎦᎵᏍᏗᏍᎪᎢ.
Transliteration: gohidi udihlehgv yigane unvdi juhnajosdi nigalsdisgoi.
English: If milk is left out in the heat too long‚ it becomes *sour.*
Tone: go:hǐ:di u:di:hlehgv̋: yigane̋: unv̌:di ju:hnajő:sdi nigalsdi:sgő:’i.

English: The boy’s *clothes* are being washed for him.
The wind is from the *north.*
He’s putting up *barbed* wire fence around the pasture.
His friend became *a bull*fighter.
Is it *smoky* because of grass fires?
They say he was asphyxiated by *smoke.*
I almost stepped on a *copperhead.*
The sunshine is very *blinding.*
This man was bitten by an *alligator.*
My mother has an old *cast iron pot.*
If he was *brave*‚ he wouldn’t have run away.
He talks to *deaf* people in sign language.
He likes to fish for *catfish.*
Where did you put my *striped* shirt?
Do you work *nightly?*
The *workers* are on a noon break.
They are going to build a new *jail.*
Which *school* did you attend?
My friend is employed at a *restaurant.*
She does her laundry on *Fridays.*
How long were you in the *hospital?*

Syllabary: ᏐᏁᎳᏚ ᎢᏍᎪᎯᏥᏆ ᎦᎵᏆᏍᎪ ᏅᎩ ᎤᏕᏘᏴᏌᏗᏒ ᎢᏤ ᏧᏂᏒᏍᏗ ᎤᎾᏁᏍᎨᎲ ᎠᏂᏣᎳᎩ ᏓᎵᏆ ᎾᎥᎢ.
Transliteration: sohneladu isgohitsgwa gahlgwasgo nvhgi udetiyvsadisv ije junisvsdi unahnesgehv anijalagi daligwa navi.
English: In the year 1974‚ the Cherokees built a new *motel* near Tahlequah.
Tone: sóhne:lá:du isgő:hitsgwa gahlgwâsgǒ: nvhgi u:de:tiyv́:sadí:sv̋: ije̋: ju:ni:sv:sdi̋: ù:na:hné:sge:hv́ ani:jalagi daligwa na’v̋:’i.

English: He went on a vacation for ten *days.*
I am going to my friend’s *house.*
Is Satan from *hell?*
Groundhog day is in the month of *February.*
I like strong *coffee.*
The bucket is *full* of water.
*Locusts* make good posts.
I grew up near *Locust Grove.*
She bought *sugar* to make *candy.*
What *month* is your birthday?
There’s a *butterfly* on your shirt.
They’re hunting wild *elephants.*
There are *wasps* flying around outside.
*He* barely *ascended* the mountain.
His car stalled going *uphill.*
Put *anger* aside.
*He angered* the wasps by hitting them with rocks.
*A spider* is making a web.
He cut *his* little *toe* with an axe.
*He’s rooting out* bushes.
Bring *a pick* when you come.
*I get cold* when it’s cold outside.
*Did he answer* when you were speaking to him‚ or not?
*He pried* the stump with a crowbar.
*He increased* his work load when he finished school.
The patient’s fever *is increasing.*
I thought *I mentioned it.*
I guess it’s heating up because *there’s foreign matter in* the water line.
Who *put foreign matter in* the well?
These *boxes* are empty.
A young bird fell out of *a nest.*
In the summertime‚ the boys haul *hay.*
She had surgery on *her knee* last May.
*I’m going to collect from him* what he owes me when I see him.
What do you kill *rats* with?
He’s eating a chicken *wing.*
He broke *his arm* when he fell.
The newscaster is telling the *news.*
A preacher preached the *gospel* to the people.
Don’t *tell* what happened.
*Did you omit him* on purpose?

Syllabary: ᏦᎢ ᎢᏯᏅᏗ ᎢᏳᏓᎵ ᎧᏃᎯᏰᎩ ᎠᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᏍᎪ ᎠᏂᏣᎳᎩ ᏗᎾᏓᏬᏍᎩ.
Transliteration: joi iyanvdi iyudali kanohiyegi analsdayvhvsgo anijalagi dinadawosgi.
English: Cherokee Baptists eat *Passover Supper* every three months.
Tone: jo’i iyánv̋:di iyú:da̋:li kano:hí:ye̋:gi à:nalsdâ:yv:hvsgó: ani:jalagi di:nada:wő:sgi.

English: The mother *is fanning* her baby.
Do you like to drink *sassafras* tea?
I want to pick *blackberries.*
He’s shooting *bullfrogs* with arrows.
*It was misting* when I came in.
*She strains* sassafras tea with a cloth.
Which *room* is the patient in?
A medicine man *cured* the boy’s earache.
A welder *is welding* a hole in a cast iron pot.

Syllabary: ᎧᏬᏂ ᏔᎵᏁ‚ ᏐᏁᎳᏚ ᎢᏍᎪᎯᏥᏆ ᎤᎩᏍᎪ ᏑᏓᎵ ᎠᏆᏕᏁᎢ.
Transliteration: kawohni taline sohneladu isgohitsgwa nvhksgo sudal agwadehnei.
English: I was born *April* second‚ nineteen hundred forty six.
Tone: kawo:hní tali:ne̋ sóhne:lá:du isgǒ:hítsgwa nvhksgǒ: sǔ:dal à:gwade:hné:’i.

Syllabary: Ꮎ ᎠᏧᏣ ᎤᏓᏅᎯᏓ ᎤᏓᎾᏩᏍᏔᏅ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏍᏓᏯ ᎧᏬᎳᏕᎠ.
Transliteration: na achuja udanvhida udanawstanv iyusdi sdaya kawoldea
English: The boy *is breathing* hard because he ran a long distance.
Tone: ná achű:ja udanv:hi̋:da udanâ:wstanv̋: iyű:sdi sda̋:ya kawoldé:’a

English: *Ducks* like the water.
*His nose* is peeling due to the hot sun.

Syllabary: ᎯᎳ ᎢᏴ ᏥᎨᏒ ᎤᏮᏕᏤ ᎠᏂᏁᎵ? Ꮭ ᏯᏆᏅᏖ ᏁᎲᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ.
Transliteration: hila iyv jigesv uwhdeje anineli hla yagwanhte nehvna gesvi.
English: When did her husband *part with her?* I didn’t know he was not living.
Tone: hilá: iyv̋: jigè:sv̋: ù:whdé:jé: ani:ne̋:li hla yagwá:nhté ne:hv̋:na gè:sv̋:’i.

English: *I use* a cane when I walk around.
*Put out* the fire‚ it’s too hot.
The lightning *put* the lights *out.*
*Are you* still *sharpening* the axe?
When the wood burned up‚ the fire *went out.*
They always put paper into the fire.
*She* likes *to mingle* with a lot of people.
My friend *is a member* of the Tahlequah police force.
He’s sending his *camel* to bed.
His chain broke but *he linked it* back together.
I’ll go to town *later.*
I went to sleep *early *last night.
I have *just* closed the window.
We’ll get there *pretty soon.*
When he was wounded‚ a doctor was there *immediately.*
Can *someone* tell me what’s happening?
*Chipmunks* live in the woods.
When she took the sheet out of the dryer‚ *she shook it.*
*The Spades* are my cousins.
*Crows* are corn thieves.
I saw him *a while ago.*
Wait for me. I’ll be back *after a while.*
Dogs like to eat *bones.*
Take the *ashes* out of the stove.
His pants became *dusty* when he fell.
Where *does* the dipper *hang?*
When I returned from hunting‚ *I hung up* my gun.
Birds like to eat *mulberries.*
Lightning struck *a sycamore* tree.
He sells a lot of *huckleberries* in the summer.
My brother works at *Pryor.*
What are you planning to do *July* 4th?
I have *a coon* dog for sale.
He thought he was hiding‚ but he was standing *in view.*
This farmer raises *peaches* and sells them.
We saw a six-foot *cobra.*

Syllabary: ᎪᎩ ᏱᏄᎵᏍᏔᎾ ᏆᏄᎾᏍᏗ ᏕᎦᏕᏍᎪ ᎡᏥ ᎠᎴ ᏕᎦᎶᏗᏍᎪᎢ.
Transliteration: gogi yinulstana kwanunsdi degadesgo eji ale degalhdisgoi.
English: Every summer my mother picks *plums* and cans them.
Tone: go:gi yinű:lstana kwanú:nsdi de:gádé:sgó e:ji alé de:gálhdǐ:sgó:’i.

English: You can hear *locusts* in the heat of the summer.
*Here!* Here is the money I owe you.
Where did you put *those* wheels?
You’re not going until *then?*
*There are* five workers.
Go stand *near* him.
Do you know how to make *hominy?*
*All* the people heard him speak.
Have you already forgotten *what he said?*
A cat *is meowing.*
He’s *always* playing ball during the summer.
*Take* your watch *off* before you dive into the water.
*He put* a lock on the door.
He wants to talk to *you.*
She says she hurts *all over.*
She likes to go *everywhere.*
He wants you two to go *now.*
They might come tomorrow *and then* they might not.
They make lumber out of *pine.*
There are a lot of *stars* out tonight.

Syllabary: ᏕᎦᎳᏅᏛ ᏱᏚᏁᏍᏓᎳᏓ ᏃᏯ ᎠᏂᎳᎨᏯᏛᏍᎪᎢ ᏅᏃᎯ ᏧᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎯ.
Transliteration: degalanvdv yidunesdalada noya anilageydvsgoi nvnohi junilvhwisdanehi.
English: When the streets freeze over‚ the road maintenance crews dump *sand* on them.
Tone: degalanvdv yidunesdalada noya anilageydvsgo’i nvnohi junilvhwisdanehi.

English: Willy has a *different* car.
*You are* really *crazy.*
Tell him to come *as quickly as possible.*
*Hurry‚* or we’ll be late.
*Come here.* I want to tell you something.
He has twenty acres of *potatoes* planted.
What do *you have on* your arm?
When are you going to *New York?*

Syllabary: ᎢᎦ ᎡᎯ ᏅᏓ ᎤᏔᎾ ᏏᏅ ᏒᏃᏱ ᎡᎯ ᏅᏓ ᎨᏒᎢ.
Transliteration: iga ehi nvda utana sihnv svnoyi ehi nvda gesvi.
English: The *sun* is bigger than the *moon.*
Tone: i:ga e̋:hi nv:da u:tana si:hnv́ sv:nǒ:yi e̋:hi nv:da gè:sv̋:’i.

English: It starts getting cold in the month of *November.*
Where is a *calendar?*
I went to *Texas* during my vacation.
Do you believe that man has been *on the moon?*
He runs *four* miles every morning.

Syllabary: ᎫᏰᏉᏂ ᏅᎩᏁ ᏙᎢ ᎠᏁᏙᎰ ᎢᎦᏓ ᏧᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎯ.
Transliteration: kuyegwoni nvhgine toi anedoho igada junilvsdanehi.
English: Some workers have a day off on the *Fourth* of July.
Tone: kuye:gwǒni nvhgi:ne̋: tő:’i à:ně:dô:hó i:gá:da junilv:sdà:ne̋:hi.

English: Come back on *Thursday.*
Someone is walking in the middle of the *road.*
Let’s live in *peace.*
I want *medicine* for a cold.
Don’t drink that *rubbing alcohol.*

Syllabary: ᏗᎦᎨᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏗᏌᎳᏙᏗ ᏅᏯ ᎩᎶ ᏃᏍᏕᎵᏍᎬᎾ ᏱᎩ.
Transliteration: digagedi gesv disaldodi nvya kilo nosdelisgvna yigi.
English: The *rocks* were too heavy to lift if no one helped.
Tone: diga:ge̋:di gè:sv̋ disaldo:di̋ nv̀:ya kilő no:sdé:lì:sgv̋:na yi̋gi.

English: He lives in a house made of *bricks.*
She lives at a place called *Rocky* Mountain.
He made a garden in a *rocky place*
Eagles live high atop the *mountains.*

Syllabary: ᎣᎦᎳᎰᎹ ᏥᎩ ’ᎩᎦᎨ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ’ ᎠᏗᎭ ᎠᏂᏣᏓ ᎤᏂᏬᏂᎯᏍᏗ.
Transliteration: oklahoma jigi gigage asgaya adiha anijahda uniwonihisdi.
English: *Oklahoma* means ’red man’ in the Choctaw language.
Tone: o:klaho:ma ji̋gi gǐ:gáge̋ asgaya à:dǐ:ha ani:jahda u:ni:wò:ni̋:hisdi.

English: That man traps *groundhogs.*
Hand me the face *soap.*
He came *afterwards.*
Stand *behind* me.
This meat is *good* to eat.
He speaks *well.*
He’s a preacher of the *gospel.*
I am doing *fairly well.*
Where shall I set this *stove?*
She won *a* new *range* in a contest.
He’s cutting wood for *a heating stove.*
Do you feel *all right?*
*Hello.* Are you well?
He catches rabbits with *traps.*
Count from *one* to twenty.
He’s wearing a *blue* cap.
Possums like to eat *persimmons.*
I hooked *a snapping turtle* while I was fishing at the pond.
I shot *a squirrel* with a .22 rifle.
*Geese* head south when it turns winter.
This rock is *hard.*
He’s working *hard.*
They have a swing made of *rope.*
They were *a little* tired from playing ball.
Close the *gate* when the cows go by.
*A key* is for unlocking a door.

Syllabary: ᏎᏓᏂ ᏗᏣᏠᏒ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᏱᎩ ᎪᎯ ᎢᎦ‚ ᎡᎵᏊ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᏱᏍᏓᎢ.
Transliteration: sedani dijadlosv nigesvn yigi kohi iga eligwu ijulaha yisdai.
English: If you haven’t been confronted by *Satan* today‚ it’s possible you two are walking in the same direction.
Tone: sědáni dijadlo:sv̋ nigě:sv̋:n yígi kò:hi i:ga e:li̋:gwu i:jú:là:ha̋ yisdá:’i.

Syllabary: ᎢᎦᏓ ᏗᏎᎯᏍᏗ ᏎᏗ ᎠᏓ ᏗᎪᏢᏔᏅ ᎨᏐᎢ.
Transliteration: igada disehisdi sedi ada digohlvhtanv gesoi.
English: Some dressers are made of *walnut.*
Tone: igá:da dì:se:hísdi̋: se:di ada digo:hlvhtanv̋ gè:só:’i.

English: It’s difficult to see *a green snake* in the grass.

Syllabary: ᎯᎳ ᎢᎦ ᏎᎷ ᏕᎭᏛᎯᏍᏓ ᎪᎯ ᎪᎩ?
Transliteration: hila iga selu dehatvhisda kohi gogi?
English: How much *corn* did you raise this summer?
Tone: hilá: iga̋: sě:lú de:hátvhísda kò:hi go:gi?

English: That farmer is planting some *popcorn.*
He accidentally spilled a sack of *corn meal.*
He’s burning some dry *corncobs.*

Syllabary: ᏍᎪᎯ ᎢᏳᏩᏂᎸ ᏂᏚᎵᏏᏂᏒ ᎦᎦᏙᏍᏗᏍᎪ ᎧᏃᎮᏓ ᎠᏓᏴᎳᏛᏍᎩ ᎠᎩᎲᎢ.
Transliteration: sgohi iyuwahnilv nidulsihnisv gagatosdisgo kanohesgv adayvlatvsgi agihvi.
English: I watch the news at *ten* o’clock each night on my television set.
Tone: sgǒ:hi iyúwa:hnilv̋: nidù:lsihnî:sv̋ gagà:to:sdí:sgó kano:hě:sgv̋: adayv̋:latvsgi à:gihv̋:’i.

English: He’s coming back the *tenth* of next month.
There were about a *hundred* people.

Transliteration: na diniyohli sgwahlesdi ju anelhdohdi unaduliha.
English: The children want to play *ball.*
Tone: ná di:ni:yő:hli sgwà:hlé:sdi ju: a:né:lhdohdi̋: ù:nadulǐ:ha.

English: He went to *New Mexico* while on his vacation.
The boys fought with their *fists.*

Syllabary: ᎪᎯ ᎢᎦ ᏓᎩᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎭ‚ ᏑᎾᎴᏅ ᎥᏍᏊ ᏙᏛᎩᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎵ.
Transliteration: kohi iga dagilvhwisdaneha sunale nasgwu dodvgilvhwsdaneli.
English: I am working today‚ and I will work tomorrow *also.*
Tone: kò:hi iga dà:gilv̌:hwísdà:ně:ha suná:le̋: nà:sgwu do:dv:gílv̂:hwsdà:ně:li.

English: Isn’t he back *yet?*
He went to his sister’s home for *one day.*
I hear *katydids.*

Syllabary: ᎡᎯ ᎠᏆᏛᎦᏅ ᏏᏊ ᏗᎦᏬᏂᏏᏒ ᎪᎯ ᎢᎦ.
Transliteration: svhi awktvganv sigwu digawonisisv kohi iga.
English: I heard yesterday that he was supposed to speak *again* today.
Tone: sv:hi̋ à:wktv:gâ:nv́ si:gwú digawó:ni:sí:sv̋: kò:hi i:ga.

English: I want *more* water.
How much does that *pig* weigh?

Syllabary: ᎢᏤ ᏓᏆᎴᎳ ᏓᏤᏝ ᏏᏅ ᎤᏪᏘ ᎨᏒᎢ.
Transliteration: ije dagwalela dajehla sihnv uweti gesvi.
English: A new car is better *than* an old one.
Tone: ije̋: dagwalé:la dà:jě:hla si:hnv́ uwe̋:ti gè:sv̋:’i.

English: They took *one month* in building the house.
He’s standing on the *other side* fishing.
Did you have to go *overseas* when you were in the service?
They’re racing *horses.*
He’s plowing with a *mule.*
He picks *hickory nuts* in the fall.
I have to see the doctor tomorrow at *nine* o’clock.
Do you want to be the *ninth?*
He went in *another* room.
Give me *another one.*
Set this chair *in the next room.*
I want to say *one thing.*

Syllabary: ᏑᏓᎵ ᎢᏳᏩᏂᎸ ᏑᎾᎴ ᏗᏜ ᎤᏰᎢᏍᏗᏍᎪᎢ ᏩᏥ.
Transliteration: sudali iyuwahnilv sunale didla uyesdisgoi waji.
English: The alarm clock wakes him up at *six* o’clock in the morning.
Tone: sǔ:dáli iyúwa:hnilv̋: suná:le̋: dí:dla ù:yê:sdi:sgó:’i wǎ:ji.

English: He hasn’t been here since the *sixth* of last month.
I bought this watch *one year* ago.
After she made the *dough*‚ she put it in the oven.
I want two *fishhooks.*

Syllabary: ᏔᎵ ᎢᏳᏟᎶᏓ ᎤᎯᎵᏙᎸ ᏑᏟᎶᏓ ᎧᏄᎦᏟ ᎦᏕᏍᎬᎢ.
Transliteration: tali iyuhliloda uhilidolv suhliloda kanugahla gadesgvi.
English: It took him two hours to pick *a gallon* of blackberries.
Tone: tá’li iyú:hli:lő:da ù:hí’lǐ:dô:lv́ su:hli:lő:da kanu:gahla gadé:sgv̋:’i.

English: The *buzzards* are eating something dead.

Syllabary: ᏑᎾᎴ ᏔᎵ ᏳᏩᏂᎸ ᏥᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᏓᏒᏍᏗ ᏗᏠᎯᏍᏗᎢ.
Transliteration: sunale tali yuwahnilv jigesesdi dasvsdi didlohisdii.
English: Our meeting is scheduled for *tomorrow* at two o’clock.
Tone: suná:le̋: tá’li yúwa:hnilv̋ jigè:se̋:sdi dà:sv:sdi di:dlo:hisdi’i.

English: Come in the *morning.*
I’ll see you *tomorrow evening.*
Stay *overnight* the next time you come.
The *barrel* filled up with rainwater when it rained.
When she peels *onions*‚ it makes her cry.
Where were you *yesterday?*
I came over for a while *yesterday morning.*
I finished bailing hay *yesterday evening.*
Did it rain *night before last?*
He mows the grass *in the evening.*
He bought some *apples* in town.
*One week* is not long.
*At midnight* I will leave.
He bought *two* pairs of shoes.
My birthday is on April *2nd.*
Are you going to work on *Tuesday?*
*Cans* get rusty if water is left in them too long.
Is your brother *a welder?*
He ran out of *solder.*
What do you make *baskets* out of?
How many *tomatoes* do you want?
*A hawk* is sitting on a post.
You can see a lot of *toads* by a pond.
If people don’t wash their hair‚ they get *lice.*
A blacksnake caught a *lizard.*
Have you ever seen *an eel?*
I heard that you weren’t *well.*
I am *just fine*‚ and you?
When he told them to be *still*‚ they became quiet.

Syllabary: ᏙᏧᏩ ᎤᏍᏗᎧ ᏏᏅ Ꮎ ᏜᏴᎦ ᎨᏒᎢ.
Transliteration: tojuhwa usdika sihnv na dlayhga gesvi.
English: The *redbird* is smaller than the bluejay.
Tone: tò:ju:hwa ù:sdí:ka si:hnv́ ná dlà:yhga gè:sv̋:’i.

English: In the summer you can see a lot of *grasshoppers.*
Do you believe there are *ghosts?*
Look! There’s *a bug* on the bush.

Syllabary: ᏥᏆᎴᏆᎵ ᏂᎦᏪᏍᎬ ᎦᏛᎩᏍᎪ ᏧᎵᏏᏅᏓ ᎠᏓᏁᎸ ᎾᎥᎢ.
Transliteration: tsgwalegwali nigawesgv gatvgisgo julsihnvda adanelv navi.
English: I hear a *whippoorwill* every night close to the house.
Tone: tsgwalé:gwalí nigawè:sgv̋: gà:tv:gî:sgó ju:lsíhnv̋:da à:da:né:lv̋: na’v̋:’i.

English: She talks *a lot* when she feels well.
I see *many* white men.
She had her *tonsils* taken out at the hospital.
He’s putting medicine on his *boil.*
What kind of *beans* are these?
Close the door‚ you’re letting the *flies* in.
*He* was asked *to pray.*
I guess *he* really *loved her.*
I didn’t know he was an *ordained minister.*
Which is the *oldest* one?
He’s really a *reckless* boy.

Syllabary: ᎪᎦ ᏂᎦᏪᏍᎬ ᏯᏛᎦᎾ ᎤᏒ‚ ᎤᏓᎴᎯ ᎠᎾᏗᏍᎪ ᎠᏂᏣᎳᎩ.
Transliteration: koga nigawesgv yatvgana usv udalehianadisgo anijalagi.
English: Cherokees say that if you hear a crow at night‚ it’s *a bad omen.*
Tone: kó:ga nigawè:sgv̋ yátv̋:gà:na usv̋ udalě:hiá:nadi:sgó: ani:jalagi.

English: *His wife* is my cousin.
That boy is really *snobbish.*
What did the *store owner* give you?
The old woman’s skin *is wrinkled.*
A bird is bathing in *a puddle* of water.
*His heart* stopped on him.
*I feel* all right now.
*He is* very *pleasant* to talk with.
He has a *tamed* raccoon.
He always keeps his room *clean.*
He walked *a long way.*
He’s walking between two *rows* of potatoes.
Breaking the law leads to *punishment.*
The workers are resting in *the shade.*
Something *is in view* in the bushes.

Syllabary: ᏑᎾᎴ ᎤᏟ ᏫᏣᏕᏒ Ꮎ ᎤᏲ ᎦᎵᏛᎢ.
Transliteration: sunale udli wijadesv na uyo galhdvi.
English: Tomorrow *throw* the trash *away.*
Tone: suná:le̋ udlí wijadê:sv́ ná u:yő: galhdv̋:’i.

English: How many *pitchers* do you all have?
He’s causing *embarrassment* by doing that.
She has a *bashful* boy.
Can you help me get my car out of *a ditch?*
Today my brother *is having a birthday.*
Tighten those *bolts.*
It’s really *hot* today.
The patient *had a* high *fever.*
Where *is he hiding?*
When he got some medicine‚ *he got well* fast.
His shirt was so small *his navel* was showing.
*There is* a job *opening* at the clothing manufacturers.
*I’ll have time* to come to visit you next month.
*Don’t you believe* I can do it?
How many *nonbelievers* are here?
*He took revenge* when he was cheated.
Go *away.*
*She’s wearing earrings* made of gold.
Who would admit that he’s *a hypocrite?*
*Dewberries* are similar to blackberries.
*A splinter* from a board stuck him in his foot.

Syllabary: ᎡᎯ ᏥᎨᏒ ᎠᎩᏢᎬ ᎠᏎᏅ ᎣᏏᏊ ᎢᎩ ᎪᎯ ᎢᎦ.
Transliteration: svhi jigesv akdlvgv asehno osigwu igi kohi iga.
English: *I was sick* yesterday‚ but I’m all right today.
Tone: sv:hí jigè:sv̋: à:kdlv̂:gv́: a:sé:hnó: o:sǐ:gwu i:gi kò:hi i:ga.

Syllabary: ᎯᎳ ᎢᏯᏂ ᏧᏂᏢᎩ ᎠᏂᏯᎠ ᎠᎭᏂ ᏧᏂᏢᎩᎢ?
Transliteration: hila iyani junidlvgi aniyaa ahani junidlvgii?
English: How many *patients* are in this hospital?
Tone: hilá: iyani̋: ju:ni:dlv̋:gi à:ni:yá’a áhani ju:ni:dlv̀:gi̋:’i?

English: That man acts *sophisticated.*
*Her brother* is visiting her for one week.
What does *her father* do?
He hunts *all day* when he goes hunting.
Do you go to church on *Sunday?*
Sometimes I work on *Saturday.*
He’s coming sometime *during the day.*
They’re going to start school *Monday.*
You did *very* well on your exam.
The boy is listening to *his grandfather* tell stories.
*I am standing* in the water.
What’s *his uncle*’s name?
*He wants* to rest.
*He’s willing* to help anytime.

Syllabary: ᎤᎵᎪᎲᏍᏗ ᎢᏴ ᏧᎦᎶᎦ ᏧᏓᎴᏅᏓ ᏧᎵᏑᏫᏓ ᏂᏙᏓᎦᎵᏍᏔᏂ.
Transliteration: uligohvsdi iyv jugaloga judalehnvda julsuhwida nidodagalstani.
English: In the fall *the leaves* will turn into different colors.
Tone: u:ligő:hvsdí i:yv̋ ju:galo:gá ju:dale:hnv̋:da ju:lsű:hwidâ nido:dagálstanî.

English: What kind of *soup* do you want?
These cantaloupes are really *sweet.*

Syllabary: ᎤᏙᎯᏳ ᎤᎦᎾᏩ ᏂᎦᎵᏍᏓ ᎪᎯ ᎢᎦ‚ ᏙᎧ?
Transliteration: udohiyu uganawi nulstanv kohi iga doka?
English: It sure got *warm* today‚ didn’t it?
Tone: u:do:hiyű u:ga̋:nawí nù:lstanv́ ko:hí i:gâ dő:ka?

English: Is the candidate who is speaking *a Democrat?*
*I am getting warm* a little now.
*He’s* sitting by the stove *warming himself*
The song is on the second *page.*
*He’s stingy* with his watch and won't loan it to anyone.
*Love* your wife.
That man is really *stingy.*
Indians wear *feathers* on their heads.
I didn’t get there until the *next morning.*
He gets up at *dawn* when he has to work.
Which one do you like *more?*
She sells *a lot of* milk.
If you wait too long to go‚ *you’ll be late.*
The old wood *is decaying.*
I smell something *rotten.*

Syllabary: ᎢᎦᏓ ᎤᏓᎴᎯ ᎠᏁᎵᏍᎪ ᎤᎫᎫ ᏱᏂᎦᏪᎠ ᎠᏓᏁᎸ ᎾᎥᎢ.
Transliteration: igada udalehi anelisgo u-guka yinigawea adanelv navi.
English: Some people think it is a bad omen to hear a *hoot owl*close to a building.
Tone: igá:da u:dale:hí à:ne:lî:sgó u:-gù:ka yinigawe̋:’a à:da:ně:lv̋ na’v̋:’i.

English: Is that man *part* Cherokee?
I want money *instead.*
Does the *Chief* know you?
*He has* ten acres of land.
*He’s yawning* because he’s sleepy.
I hear *a bell* ringing.
*His lips* are getting dry.
Open the window. *I’m smothering.*
*There’s dew* on the ground.
*She* doesn’t like to *get lonesome.*
*It was lonesome* when she left.
He became *strong* by lifting weights.
Write their names down *quickly.*
Where does *her aunt* live?
I want the *same kind of*car he has.
Are you going *there?*
Some *islands* are small.
Leave the dog alone *because he’s getting mad.*
The man *is always angry.*
He’s drinking tea made from sassafras *root.*
Don’t chop on the tree *roots.*
*He stubbed his toe* on a big rock.
She’s picking some possum *grapes.*
He’s trimming *his toenails* with a knife.
This patient *has a chill.*
He tore *his shirt* going under a fence.
He doesn’t like for anyone to see *his scar.*
Are *you* already *in bed?*

Syllabary: ᎤᏂᏡᎬᎢᏊ ᎢᎦ ᎤᏟᏃᎯᏯ Ꮎ ᎤᏔᎾ ᏥᏡᎬᎢ.
Transliteration: uhnilhgvigwu iga uhlinohiya na utana jidluhgvi.
English: *A stump* is all that’s left of the big tree.
Tone: u:hni:lhgv̋:’igwu i:ga̋: ù:hlino:hí:ya ná ű:tana jidluhgv̋:’i.

English: He charges *one dollar *when he fixes a tire.
Soldiers *wear* uniforms.
The *rainbow* faded when the cloud cleared up.
Go stand in the *corner.*
He took some *thick* clothing when he went up north.
Yesterday *I bought* a car.
That woman’s *husband* is looking for her.
*He is looking for* the watch he lost yesterday.
What’s *floating* on the water?
He has a big *horn* on his car.

Syllabary: ᎠᏣᎳᎩ ᎤᏴᏜ ’ᏳᏂᏴᎭ’ Ꮭ ᎤᏴᏜ ᏱᎦᏂᏱᏍᎪᎢ‚ ᎤᏴᏜᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᏐ ᎤᏂᏱᏍᎪᎢ.
Transliteration: ajalagi uhyvdla yuniyvha hla uhyvdla yiganiyisgoi uhyvdlasgini ugvwiyuso uniyisgoi.
English: Whenever a Cherokee ’catches’ a *cold*‚ he doesn’t catch the *cold*; rather‚ the *cold* catches him.
Tone: ajalagi u:hyv:dla ’yú:ni:yv̋:ha’ hla u:hy:vdla yigá’ni:yǐ:sgó:’i u:hyv:dlásgini u:gv:wiyú:só: ù:ní:yi:sgó:’i.

English: I’m thirsty for *cold* water.
Do you vote *Republican?*
I don’t feel good. *I have a cold.*
Don’t *choke on it.*
How many did he drink before *he got drunk?*
I see *a drunk* man walking this way.
Some *drunkard* froze to death last winter.
He likes to drink *strong* coffee.
The cat *is inside* the box.
They make bread out of *wheat.*
A drunk was arrested among *a lot *of people.
*His* car is red.
When you say something to him‚ *he grins.*
I ran over *a possum* last night.
Doesn’t he hear *his mother* calling him?
Have you ever picked *cotton?*
Some fishermen use *worms* when they fish.
When he heard a snake *rattling* he froze.
*Rattlesnakes* live in that cave over there.
His writing is *confusing.*

Syllabary: ᏁᎳᎩ ᏧᎦᎿᏅ ᏒᎯ‚ ᎦᏳᎳ ᎤᎧᏲᏓ ᎢᎩ ᎦᏙᎯ.
Transliteration: nelagi jugahnanv svhi gayula ukayoda igi gadohi.
English: Although it rained yesterday‚ the ground is already *dry.*
Tone: ne̋:lagi jù:ga:hnanv̋: sv:hí gá:yu:la u:kayő:da ǐ:gi ga:dő:hi.

English: Walk on *a dry place.*
*His face* is just hot.
His house is in the *valley.*
What’s sticking out of your pocket?
*He’s watching* a prisoner so he won’t get away.
What do *goats* eat?
*diamondback rattlers* are dangerous.
She saves watermelon *seeds.*
*It’s foggy* this morning.
Kittens are *cute.*
He went deer hunting yesterday but *he had no luck.*
*His feet* are sticking out from under his covers.
He’s lacing up *his shoes.*
*He got lost* because he didn’t know which way to go.
He became *skinny* because he was sick for a long time.
*You’ll be making a mistake* if you say that.
He’s going to start school in the *fall.*
Is that snake *dead?*
My brother has *curly* hair.
He’s taking the *fat* off the meat.
She’s cleaning some hog *intestines.*
He’s not too *weak.*
*His grandmother* gave him the coat.
Why *is he moaning?*
*She was wearing* a wedding *ring.*
*It’s cloudy* like it’s going to rain.
The *clouds* are moving fast.

Syllabary: ᎪᏍᏗ ᎤᎵᏍᏓᏁᎴ Ꮲ ᏪᏙᎲ. Ꮭ ᏱᎤᎷᏤᎢ.
Transliteration: gohusdi ulsdahnele dlv wedohv hla yiuluhjei.
English: Something *happened to him* while he was away. He didn’t come back.
Tone: go:hű:sdi ù:lsdâ:hně:lê dlv̋: wě:dô:hv̋ hla yi’u:lúhjé:’i.

English: What’s *wrong with* the dog?
When I found out the door was *open*‚ I went in.
Those ball players *are wearing* new caps.
I guess it wasn’t *important.*
I found a *broken* broom.
He says *he’s broke.*

Syllabary: ᎩᎶ ᏦᎳᎾ ᎤᎵᏍᏆᎷᏓ ᎠᏎᏃ Ꮭ ᏯᏆᏅᏔ ᎥᏍᎩ ᎨᏒᎢ.
Transliteration: kilo jolana ulsgwaluda asehno hla yagwanhta vsgi gesvi.
English: Someone *is sticking his head out* of the window‚ but I don’t know who it is.
Tone: kilő: jő:lana ù:lsgwalú:da a:sé:hnó: hla yagwá:nhta v̀:sgi gè:sv̋:’i.

English: Every time I see him‚ *he’s wearing a* different *hat.*
It’s really *dark* tonight.

Syllabary: ᏍᏓᏱ ᎠᎪᏩᏛᏗ ᎤᎵᏏᎬ ᎩᎶ ᎤᎵᏏᎩ ᏳᏄᏩ.
Transliteration: sdayi agohwahtvhdi ulsihgv kilo ulsihgi yuhnuwa.
English: It’s hard to see someone in the dark if he has on a *dark* shirt.
Tone: sda̋:yi ago:hwahtvhdi̋: ulsihgv̋: kilő: ű:lsihgi yű:hnuwa.

English: I have a yellow *colored* car.
I’m not allergic to *poison ivy.*
*I like her* because she’s friendly.

Syllabary: ᏑᏓᎵ ᎢᎦ ᎠᎹ ᏧᏟᏍᏙᏗ ᏧᎸᏌᏗ ᏚᏩᏒᎢ.
Transliteration: sudali iga ama judlisdohdi julvsada duhwasvi.
English: She bought six water *glasses.*
Tone: sǔ:dáli iga̋: ama jű:dlì:sdohdi ju:lv̋:sada dù:hwasv́:’i.

English: *Do you have* a driver’s license?
The *chain* broke as he was pulling a car.
Let’s go to the *ball field.*
My friend and I went to Texas *together.*
I am going to go and visit *my friend.*
I saw her with *her boy friend* awhile ago.
Are you going to get a *plaid* shirt?

Syllabary: ᎩᎬᎭ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅ ᎤᏁᎦ ᎤᏄᏮ ᎩᎦ ᏚᏴᏍᏉᎳ.
Transliteration: gigvha nulstanv unega uhnuwv giga duhyvsgwola.
English: His *white* shirt got bloody when his nose started bleeding.
Tone: gi:gv:ha̋ nù:lstanv́ u:né:ga u:hnuwv́ gǐ:ga dù:hyv̋:sgwǒla.

English: *His skin* is just burned.
He was hiding in an *ugly* house.
Quit. *You’re being* too *mean.*
Watch yourself‚ they have a *mean* dog.
I forgot to bring *a blanket.*
I always *have* milk to sell.

Syllabary: ᏱᎰᏩ ᏚᏙᎠ ᎤᏁᏝᏅᎯ ᎠᏗᎭ ᎦᎸᏉᏗ ᎪᏪᎵ.
Transliteration: yihowa dudoa unehlanvhi adiha galvkwdi gohweli.
English: The Bible says *God*’s name is Jehovah.
Tone: yihǒ:wa dù:dó’a u:ne:hlánv:hi à:dǐ:ha galv̋:kwdi gohwelí.

English: He went to town after some *ice.*
The *snow* stayed on the ground for a long time.
*Did he know* that you were supposed to come today?
His *grandfather* raised him.
He’s shearing the *sheep* with shears.
A bell *rings* when it’s time for church to start.
Something *is making noise* in the wheel.
*A tornado* went through a big city.
The month of *January* is the beginning of a new year.
He likes to drink *milk.*
How big is the human *brain?*
He has on *thin* covers.
She’s ironing all of *her dresses.*
When he entered the *cave*‚ they didn’t see him a second time.
Are there any *crackers?*
He cooked for *his wife* on her birthday
I hear a little *baby* crying.

Syllabary: ᏧᏍᏗ ᏓᏆᎴᎳ ᎤᎪᏗ ᏓᎩᎸᏉᏗ ᏏᏅ ᏧᏔᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ.
Transliteration: jusdi dagwalela ugodi dagilvgwdi sihnv jutana gesvi.
English: I like *small* cars better than big ones.
Tone: jusdi̋: dagwalé:la ű:gò:di da:gilv̌:gwdi si:hnv jű:tana gè:sv̋:’i.

English: He got lost walking through a *trail.*
He’s scratching *his scalp.*
What *is he being punished* for?
He *barely* lifted the heavy rock.
Some people are *slow* to get ready.
Today seemed to go by *slow.*
The car has *bright* lights.
They talk about *interesting* things when they talk.
He’s rubbing *his stomach.*
Hand me an *empty* can.
*He’s coughing* because he smokes too much.
He found a human *skull* while digging.

Syllabary: ᏡᎬ ᎤᏍᎪᎵ ᏭᎶᏢ ᏌᎶᎵ ᎩᏟ ᎤᎨᎮᎾ.
Transliteration: dluhgv uskoli wulohlv saloli gihli ukehena.
English: The squirrel climbed to the *top* of the tree when the dog chased it.
Tone: dluhgv̋: u:skő:li wù:lô:hlv́ salǒ:li gi:hli ù:ke:he̋:na.

English: He didn’t even see me standing *behind him.*
*Her hair* is always clean.
The cat’s *claws* are sharp.
Didn’t he know that *he was wearing* his pants inside out?
I wonder where he buys *his pants.*
He’s cleaning his *shotgun.*

Syllabary: ᎠᎹ ᏧᏪᏓᏍᏗ ᏧᏒᏙᏂ ᏚᏁᏍᏓᎳᏛ ᎤᏒ ᏥᎨᏒᎢ.
Transliteration: ama juwedasdi jusvdoni dunesdaladv usv jigesvi.
English: The water *pipes* froze last night.
Tone: ama juwedà:sdi̋: ju:sv:dő:ni dù:ně:sdálá’dv́ usv̋: jigè:sv̋:’i.

English: When *are you going to bed?*
My friend works during *nights.*
*I am thirsty* for water.
When he was called names‚ *he became enraged.*
He is building a *big* house.
He got burned by *steam.*
They’re eating *leftovers* from yesterday.
He broke *his ankle* when he jumped from a tree.
I don’t like a *crooked* arrow.
It rained *a lot* yesterday.
I *hope* to hear from her.
I like *to listen* to thunder.
Who is the *old chap?*
When a man becomes fully grown‚ they call him an *old man.*
Someone is hiding behind the *bushes.*

Syllabary: ᎦᏙ ᎤᏍᏗ ᎲᏗᏍᎪ ᏅᏅᏕᏍᎬ ᎠᏣᏗ ᎤᏩᏥᏍᎦᏢᎢ?
Transliteration: gado usdi hvhdisgo hnvnhdesgv ajadi uwajisgahlvi
English: What do you use to take fish *scales* off?
Tone: gado u:sdi hvhdi:sgô: hnv́:nhde:sgv̋: ajadi uwa:ji:sgahlv̋:’i

English: Just *forget about it.*
Whenever he eats a lot‚ *he belches.*
*She’s shivering* because she’s cold.
Do you have any *ripe* cantaloupes?
She’s wearing a sweater made from *wool.*
Owls like to sit on *limbs.*
Why *are you in* such *a hurry?*
He came *by himself.*
*Only* he knows what happened.
His hands became *numb* when they became cold.
He says *he smells* something strong.
*I have* a shotgun.
When he applauds‚ his hand *hurts.*
Wolves *howled* all night last night.
How did he become *wealthy?*
How much are a dozen *eggs?*
He’s going to go visit *his sons.*
He’s looking for *his son.*

Syllabary: Ꮎ ᎤᏓᏥ ᎤᏪᏥᏅ ᎠᎨᏯ ᎤᏂᏠᏱ ᏗᎨᎦᎦᏅᏗᎢ.
Transliteration: na udaji uwejihnv agehya unitloyi digegaganhdii.
English: The mother and *her daughter* look alike.
Tone: ná u:da:jí uwě:jihnv́ agě:hya u:ni:tlő:yi dige:gaga:nhdi̋:’i.

English: *His daughter* is graduating this summer.
Who did *his son* marry?
*He had* a mean dog.
She’s cooking *liver.*
Don’t *worry.*
He felt *worried* when he heard that his father was sick.
He has an *old* heating stove.

Syllabary: ᎠᎩᏄᎸᏅ ᏗᎩᏐᎯᏍᏗ ᎤᏪᏴᎢ ᏍᏓᏱ ᎦᏃᎱᎩᏍᎬ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ.
Transliteration: aginulvhnv digisohisdi uweyvi sdayi ganohugisgv iyusdi.
English: I couldn’t cross the *river* because it was too swift.
Tone: à:ginu:lv́:hnv́: di:gi:sohísdi̋: uwé:yv̋:’i sdà:yi gano:hu:gí:sgv̋: iyű:sdi.

English: She hung the *damp* clothes on the line.
There goes a *brown* car.
She was given a *pretty* car when she graduated.
He wants to play with *foam.*
*He’s always confident* that he’s doing the right thing.
*Have faith.*
Where *will you be sitting?*
*Is* your brother *home?*
He dived from *a bluff.*
The old man is telling *funny* stories.
My ankle is *swollen.*
He greeted him with *his hand.*
I see something *furry.*
He wants to talk with you for some *reason.*
He stopped the car *suddenly.*
He swam *naked* in the creek.
Why *are you laughing?*
*I’m itching* where something bit me.
*He lost* his watch underwater.
Why are some people afraid of *dead* people?
His bicycle broke down on a *bad* road.
He’s picking up a *broken* glass.
She has a very *spoiled* child.
I didn’t eat this morning and now *I’m hungry.*
Let’s go fishing at the *pond.*
He caught *a* bad *disease.*
Some say that *locusts* are good to eat.

Syllabary: ᏥᏈᏍᏗ ᏧᎬᏩᎶᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎦᎵᏦᏕ‚ ᎥᏍᎩᎾ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ Ꮭ ᏯᎩᏩᏎᎢ.
Transliteration: tsgwisdi jugvwalhdi gesv gahljode vsgina iyusdi hla yakiwasei.
English: The house cost too much *therefore* I didn’t buy it.
Tone: tsgwi̋:sdi ju:gv̋:walhdi gè:sv́ gahljo:de v̀:sginá iyű:sdi hla yakíwasé:’i.

English: Christmas is in the month of *December.*
What would happen if they did *that?*
*Dragonflies* fly around close to a pond.
Where do you want *me to send* this box?
*Bees* make *honey.*
He cut a *blood vessel* when he cut himself on the foot with an axe.
This man owns a lot of *cows.*
I like to eat *beef.*
I know a girl whose last name is *Owl.*

Syllabary: ᎥᏍᎩᏱ ᏔᎵᏍᎪ ᏅᎩᏁ ᏭᏅ ᏓᏂᏍᏓᏲᎯᎲ ᎠᏓᏁᏗ.
Transliteration: vsgihyi talsgo nvhgine wuhnv danisdayohihv adahnehdi.
English: *He sent* the Christmas gift on December the 24th.
Tone: v:sgí:hyi tâlsgǒ: nvhgi:ne̋ wù:hnv́ dà:nisdayo:hihv̋ ada̋:hnehdi.

English: *Wolves* are dangerous when they’re hungry.
What time does the *clock* say?
If you swallow *saliva* while running‚ it tires you out.
*I am headed* for town.
*Hummingbirds* cannot walk.
The *frogs* are croaking.
He’s chopping on a *hickory* tree.
She likes to use a *soft* pillow.
She thought it was a *moth*‚ but it was a butterfly.
Do you know anyone named *Washington?*
Have you been to *Washington?*
Do you see *a cat?*

Syllabary: Ꮲ ᏰᎦ‚ ᎾᎥ ᎢᏧᎾᏓᎵ ᏗᎦᏁᎸ ᏫᎦᎧᎲᏍᎪ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎠᏲᏟ.
Transliteration: dlv yega nav ijunadali diganelv wigakahvsgo uweji ayohli.
English: Whenever she goes somewhere‚ *she takes* her child to her neighbor.
Tone: dlv̋ ye̋:ga na’v̋ ijú:nada̋:li digané:lv̋ wiga:káhvsgó uwě:jí ayő:hli.

English: *She took* the glass of water inside.

Syllabary: ᎠᏕᎳ ᏧᏔᏲᏎᎳ ᎤᏪᏥ ᏔᎵᏍᎪ ᎠᏕᎸ ᏭᏅᏁᎸᎢ.
Transliteration: adela utayosela uweji talsgo adelv wunvnelvi
English: When his son asked him for some money‚ *he sent him* a twenty dollar bill.
Tone: adě:la u:tayo:se̋:la uwě:ji talsgó adě:lv̋ wù:nv́:ne:lv̌:’i

English: Some *pigeons* are message carriers.

Syllabary: ᏭᏕᎵᎬ ᎢᏗᏜ ᏭᏕᎵᎪ ᎢᎦ ᎡᎯ ᏅᏓ.
Transliteration: wudeligv ididla wudeligo iga ehi nvda.
English: The sun *goes out of sight* in the west.
Tone: wǔ:dé:lì:gv̋: idí:dla wù:dé:lì:gó i:ga e̋:hi nv:da.

English: Clouds are forming in the *west.*
What do you want me to do with the *rest of* the money?
The *buffalo* is almost extinct.

Syllabary: ᏱᏂᎦᎵᏍᏗᎭᏊ ᎢᎸᏢ ᎤᏪᏅᏍᏗ ᏳᏚᎵ ᎡᎪ ᏱᏂᎦᎵᏍᏗᎭᏊ ᎢᏴᎢ.
Transliteration: yinigalsdihagwu ilvhdlv uwenvsdi yuduli ego yinigalsdihagwu iyvi.
English: He goes *wherever* he wants to go anytime.
Tone: yinigálsdi̋:hagwu ilv́hdlv̋ uwe:nv̀:sdi̋: yu:dú:li̋ ego yinigálsdi̋:hagwu iyv̋:’i.

English: You may choose *anyone* to help you.
Have you seen anyone wrestle *a bear?*
That white man is speaking *English.*
*Sometimes* things don’t turn out the way I want them to.
The handle of his hammer broke as he was driving *a nail.*
Long ago Cherokees used to tell stories about *cannibals.*
